status: Patient-value overlay for action_library.json. V1 remains the legacy source of action identity, effect size, evidence, findings, and citations. V2 is the valuation layer.
disclaimer: Prototype machinery, not clinical guidance. The purpose is to prevent payer-style scalar compression from becoming the core action-library contract.

valuation_contract.intrinsic_value_rule: Intrinsic QALY is estimated as patient-conditional value if achieved and sustained. Feasibility, adherence, cost, and patient preference are selection axes, not intrinsic-value multipliers.
valuation_contract.direct_action_rule: Direct interventions must expose C1 prevention, C2 capacity, C3 resilience, burden, and uncertainty. Honest zeroes are required.
valuation_contract.diagnostic_rule: Diagnostics must expose probability of meaningful reclassification and patient QALY if reclassified. Expected VOI remains available for internal accounting but must not replace the two displayed components.
valuation_contract.legacy_scalar_rule: qaly_per_event in action_library.json is a fallback projection only. It is not the durable patient-value schema.

channel_definitions.c1_prevention: Events, morbidity, mortality, or disease onset that do not happen.
channel_definitions.c2_capacity: Higher lived quality in baseline-survival years, including independence, reserve, vitality, and function.
channel_definitions.c3_resilience: Better survival, disability, recovery, or tolerance when disease or injury still occurs.
channel_definitions.burden_qaly: Patient-lived cost, harm, time, effort, anxiety, radiation, procedural risk, or false-positive cascade.

applies_when.interpretation: Low-activity or sedentary patient where the lever is a real fitness/activity change, not marginal optimization in an already-active patient.

bundle_valuations.patient_value_frame: Full achievable potential if the patient captures and maintains a clinically meaningful activity/fitness change. Selection feasibility is handled later.
bundle_valuations.reducer_rule: Use this bundle to value the overall fitness/activity opportunity. Final plans should select scored atomic actions when available, such as supervised resistance training or a specific aerobic prescription, and allocate shared value back to the bundle without summing both.

c1_prevention.basis: Disease incidence, cardiovascular/all-cause mortality, metabolic and activity-sensitive cancer prevention.

c2_capacity.basis: Independence threshold, reserve, energy, physical readiness, and compression of dependent years.
c2_capacity.anchor: Per-year Δu anchored to within-person exercise/intervention-RCT utility deltas (EQ-5D/SF-6D pre/post), banded and durability-checked against cross-sectional CRF-vs-QoL gradients, measured net of event-driven quality, applied per-lever. Ratified 2026-07-02 (framework §3 C2 anchoring rule, D1).

c3_resilience.basis: Better tolerance and recovery if illness still occurs.

burden_qaly.basis: Time, effort, injury risk, and sustained-behavior friction. This is not an adherence haircut.
burden_qaly.note: Per-year chronic burden integrated over the sustained horizon, survival-weighted (D4).

basis.vo2max_gain: lever size: a sustained +10 mL/kg/min VO2max change (the bundle's defined achievable target)
basis.causal_fraction: [JUDGMENT/MR] D3 causality haircut on the observational CRF-mortality gradient
basis.delta_u_per_year: [RCT within-person] EQ-5D/SF-6D exercise-RCT utility deltas (D1 anchor); cross-sectional CRF-QoL gradient sets the band
basis.dependency_years_compressed: [JUDGMENT] disability-incidence-by-CRF cohorts exist; utility mapping pending

bundle_valuations.allocation_rule: When the v2 bundle is active, allocate the one bundle value across present findings using these weights normalized to present allocated findings. Do not sum legacy per-event slices on top of this bundle value.
overlap_rules: C2 capacity is credited once for the activity/fitness lever, never once per disease domain.
C1 disease-prevention slices are included inside the bundle anchor and should not be added again from legacy physical-activity slices.
C3 resilience is credited once as a general reserve effect, not separately per downstream disease.
Do not rank the bundle itself as a patient instruction when a more atomic action is present.

cohort_projection.notes: The v1 effect sizes remain evidence descriptors. The v2 bundle total is the QALY projection consumed by the cohort engine when physical_inactivity is present.

evidence_trace.transformation: Physical activity and CRF mortality associations are imported with the D3 causality haircut and converted to C1 through the value runner.
evidence_trace.limitations: Observational dose-response evidence supports direction and scale, but residual confounding and exact patient QALY translation remain model-dependent.

citation.title: Leisure time physical activity and mortality

evidence_trace.transformation: Guideline-backed physical activity dose supports functional capacity and lived-quality channel; D1 performs the utility translation.
evidence_trace.limitations: Guidelines do not directly supply EQ-5D/SF-6D deltas or patient-specific QALY magnitudes.

citation.title: Physical Activity Guidelines for Americans, 2nd edition

evidence_trace.transformation: Exercise trial evidence for falls and function supports a resilience channel, strongest in older or fall-risk populations.
evidence_trace.limitations: Resilience effect is age/risk-dependent and not directly measured as lifetime QALY for younger preventive cohorts.

citation.title: Exercise for preventing falls in older people living in the community

evidence_trace.transformation: D4 burden cadence rule treats sustained behavior friction as per-year burden integrated over survival-weighted horizon.
evidence_trace.limitations: Magnitude still needs specific burden calibration from time/injury/adherence data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Guideline and cohort evidence strongly support direction, but C1 and C2 QALY magnitudes require causal haircut and utility translation.

applies_when.interpretation: Use when primary-prevention lipid treatment is indicated by risk, risk enhancer, or shared decision threshold and contraindications are absent.

per_event.basis: Derivation object per D2 — replaces the disputed bare scalar. Length/mortality term computed as a survival-curve shift; non-fatal term event-based. See QALY_WORKED_TRACE.md S4 and QALY_METHOD_DECISIONS.md D2.

legacy_scalar_disputed.note: NOT-YET-DEFENSIBLE (trace F1): the bare per-event scalar 0.8 has no derivation and traces 3-6x higher under the no-discount rules. Retained only for provenance; superseded by projection=survival_curve_shift + per_event.

burden_qaly.note: Per-year chronic burden of daily therapy, integrated over the sustained horizon and survival-weighted (D4). Not a total.

basis.risk_domain: [MODEL] patient 30-yr PREVENT CVD risk from packet; reference 0.16 = AL-47M anchor. The patient's CVD share of mortality is scaled by risk/reference.
basis.cvd_mortality_share_base: [TABLE] CVD share of all-cause mortality at these ages
basis.mortality_rrr: [RCT] CTT ~0.12 all-cause-mortality RRR per mmol/L LDL x ~1.27 mmol/L dLDL (moderate statin)
basis.relative_risk_reduction_events: [RCT] CTT ~22%/mmol/L major vascular events scaled to dLDL
basis.fatal_fraction: [EPI] share of first CVD events that are fatal

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: integral[ (S_treated - S_baseline) * u ] (length) + event_based nonfatal term - integral[ burden_per_year * S ]  (runner-computed per D2/D4; the legacy 'patient_risk * RRR * scalar - burden' form is superseded)

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Strong event evidence. Capacity and resilience channels are honest zeroes for routine primary-prevention statin use.
overlap_rules: Do not add a C2 healthspan benefit unless a separate patient-specific pathway is established.
Do not truncate fatal-event benefit solely because a cited endpoint is nonfatal.

evidence_trace.transformation: CTT relative effects are imported; Aleron recomputes patient-specific C1 using baseline risk, survival curve, fatal/nonfatal split, and D2 curve-shift.
evidence_trace.limitations: Trial populations/effects are population-level; patient QALY depends on accurate baseline risk and sustained therapy.

citation.title: Efficacy and safety of more intensive lowering of LDL cholesterol: a meta-analysis of data from 170,000 participants in 26 randomised trials

evidence_trace.transformation: D4 cadence treats daily therapy burden as per-year and integrates over sustained horizon.
evidence_trace.limitations: Per-year statin burden magnitude remains a provisional patient-lived-cost estimate.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: RCT meta-analysis support for lipid lowering with patient-risk scaling; burden and exact patient QALY remain model-translated but direction is strong when indicated.

applies_when.interpretation: Use only after ferritin, iron indices, anemia status, and symptom context support iron deficiency as an active driver.

c1_prevention.basis: Avoided anemia progression, reduced exercise intolerance, and lower downstream complication burden when deficiency is real.

c2_capacity.basis: Energy, physical readiness, cognitive stamina, and training tolerance improve when symptomatic deficiency is corrected.

c3_resilience.basis: Reserve benefit during illness or training load is plausible but secondary.

burden_qaly.basis: GI side effects, infusion burden if needed, monitoring, and risk of overtreatment.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Moderate certainty when true deficiency is confirmed; weak when used as empiric fatigue treatment without deficiency.
overlap_rules: Do not score unless deficiency is confirmed.
Do not double count the same vitality lift inside a broad exercise bundle.
If thyroid dysfunction is also confirmed, allocate shared fatigue benefit explicitly.

evidence_trace.transformation: Iron repletion burden is bounded by oral dosing, GI intolerance, monitoring, and IV iron only when indicated or oral therapy fails.
evidence_trace.limitations: Guidelines define management and safety burden but not a utility decrement.

citation.title: AGA Clinical Practice Update on Management of Iron Deficiency Anemia

evidence_trace.transformation: Confirmed iron deficiency or iron deficiency anemia is treated to avoid ongoing deficiency, anemia progression, and downstream morbidity.
evidence_trace.limitations: C1 QALY magnitude is model-calibrated; strongest when anemia, bleeding source, pregnancy, or comorbidity increases consequence.

citation.title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines for management of iron deficiency anaemia

evidence_trace.transformation: RCT and meta-analysis evidence shows reduced subjective fatigue in iron-deficient adults; Aleron translates fatigue improvement to C2 capacity.
evidence_trace.limitations: Heterogeneous populations; objective physical capacity and direct utility/QALY evidence are limited.

citation.title: Efficacy of iron supplementation on fatigue and physical capacity in non-anaemic iron-deficient adults

evidence_trace.transformation: Correction of confirmed deficiency plausibly improves reserve under illness or training load by restoring iron availability.
evidence_trace.limitations: Resilience endpoint is not directly trial-quantified and should remain secondary.

citation.title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines for management of iron deficiency anaemia

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Confirmed deficiency treatment is guideline-supported and fatigue benefit is trial/meta-analysis supported, but C1/C3 value is indirect.

applies_when.interpretation: Use only when repeat TSH/free T4 and clinical context support treatment or specialist-directed follow-up.

c1_prevention.basis: Avoided morbidity from untreated clinically significant thyroid dysfunction.

c2_capacity.basis: Energy, mood, cognition, thermoregulation, and physical readiness may improve when true dysfunction is corrected.

c3_resilience.basis: Secondary benefit through better physiologic stability.

burden_qaly.basis: Repeat labs, dose titration, over-replacement risk, and monitoring burden.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Moderate when overt or clinically significant hypothyroidism is confirmed; lower for borderline isolated lab variation.
overlap_rules: Do not score from a single borderline TSH alone.
Do not double count shared fatigue benefit with iron repletion or sleep treatment.
Keep overtreatment harm visible in burden.

evidence_trace.transformation: Thyroid treatment burden comes from TSH/free T4 monitoring, dose titration, and avoiding over-replacement.
evidence_trace.limitations: Guidelines specify monitoring but not QALY burden magnitude.

citation.title: Guidelines for the treatment of hypothyroidism

evidence_trace.transformation: Treatment of confirmed clinically significant hypothyroidism is standard care to avoid morbidity from untreated dysfunction.
evidence_trace.limitations: C1 magnitude depends on overt vs subclinical disease; subclinical fatigue-only treatment should not inherit overt-disease benefit.

citation.title: Guidelines for the treatment of hypothyroidism

evidence_trace.transformation: Overt hypothyroidism treatment supports symptom and capacity recovery; TRUST and meta-analysis evidence constrain benefit for most subclinical hypothyroidism.
evidence_trace.limitations: Must be stratified by overt disease, TSH level, age, pregnancy status, and symptom attribution.

citation.title: Association of thyroid hormone therapy with quality of life and thyroid-related symptoms

evidence_trace.transformation: Correcting clinically significant thyroid dysfunction supports physiologic stability as a secondary channel.
evidence_trace.limitations: Direct C3 lifetime QALY magnitude is not trial-derived.

citation.title: Clinical practice guidelines for hypothyroidism in adults

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Overt clinically significant dysfunction has guideline-backed treatment; subclinical fatigue-only benefit is constrained by negative RCT/meta-analysis evidence.

applies_when.interpretation: Use for confirmed deficiency, especially when bone health, falls risk, or muscle symptoms change the action sequence.

c1_prevention.basis: Bone and fall-risk pathway in deficiency, especially if DEXA or clinical context shows vulnerability.

c2_capacity.basis: Small possible muscle and physical-readiness benefit when deficiency is real.

c3_resilience.basis: Small reserve benefit through bone and muscle function.

burden_qaly.basis: Supplement burden, repeat testing, and hypervitaminosis risk if poorly monitored.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Lower to moderate. Deficiency correction is reasonable, but broad vitality claims without deficiency should not be credited.
overlap_rules: Do not score as a general longevity intervention.
Do not double count bone value if pharmacologic osteoporosis treatment is separately scored.
Keep the expected value small unless deficiency or bone vulnerability is confirmed.

evidence_trace.transformation: Confirmed deficiency treatment burden is low but includes supplementation, repeat testing when indicated, and avoidance of overtreatment.
evidence_trace.limitations: Screening and threshold guidance has changed; do not use this as support for broad routine testing.

citation.title: Vitamin D for the prevention of disease: Endocrine Society guideline

evidence_trace.transformation: Use C1 only for confirmed deficiency or bone-risk contexts; broad supplementation in generally healthy adults has not reduced fractures in VITAL.
evidence_trace.limitations: Does not justify broad fracture-prevention credit in unselected adults; magnitude requires patient bone-risk modeling.

citation.title: Supplemental vitamin D and incident fractures in midlife and older adults

evidence_trace.transformation: Vitamin D3 improved self-perceived fatigue in a small RCT of vitamin-D-deficient fatigued adults.
evidence_trace.limitations: Short-term subjective fatigue endpoint, not direct QALY; applicability depends on symptomatic confirmed deficiency.

citation.title: Effect of vitamin D3 on self-perceived fatigue

evidence_trace.transformation: Confirmed deficiency management can support bone and muscle reserve in targeted risk contexts.
evidence_trace.limitations: Resilience magnitude is not directly endpoint-matched; broad population screening remains unsupported.

citation.title: Vitamin D for the prevention of disease: Endocrine Society guideline

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Confirmed deficiency treatment is supported, but broad prevention/fracture and capacity value are limited and context-dependent.

applies_when.interpretation: Use when BMI and comorbidity criteria are met and contraindications, access, side effects, lean-mass protection, and durability are addressed.

c1_prevention.basis: Diabetes prevention, BP/metabolic improvement, and downstream cardiovascular-risk reduction when obesity or insulin resistance is active.

c2_capacity.basis: Body ease, energy, mobility, and relative VO2max can improve when excess adiposity falls and lean mass is protected.

c3_resilience.basis: Better reserve through lower adiposity, lower inflammation, and improved cardiometabolic tolerance.

burden_qaly.basis: GI side effects, medication burden, cost/access friction, monitoring, rare serious adverse events, and weight-regain risk if stopped.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Moderate. Strong weight and glycemic evidence, but long-horizon patient-QALY depends on durability, baseline risk, lean-mass protection, and maintenance strategy.
overlap_rules: Do not double count weight-mediated VO2max improvement already credited to physical activity.
Pair with resistance training or nutrition guardrails when lean-mass loss would reduce capacity value.
Keep adherence and medication access as feasibility axes, not intrinsic QALY haircuts.

evidence_trace.transformation: SELECT supports cardiovascular event reduction for semaglutide in overweight/obesity with established CVD; SURMOUNT-1 supports large weight-loss/metabolic effect for tirzepatide.
evidence_trace.limitations: SELECT is secondary prevention with established CVD; primary-prevention C1 must be model-derived and eligibility-gated.

citation.title: Semaglutide and cardiovascular outcomes in obesity without diabetes

evidence_trace.transformation: STEP-1 and SURMOUNT-1 provide randomized evidence for large weight loss, supporting capacity and mobility translation when lean mass is protected.
evidence_trace.limitations: Weight change is a surrogate for C2 utility; durability depends on continued therapy, access, side effects, and resistance training/nutrition.

citation.title: Tirzepatide once weekly for the treatment of obesity

evidence_trace.transformation: SELECT supports event reduction and cardiometabolic risk improvement in eligible established-CVD obesity patients, used as resilience support where applicable.
evidence_trace.limitations: General primary prevention resilience remains extrapolated.

citation.title: Semaglutide and cardiovascular outcomes in obesity without diabetes

evidence_trace.transformation: Semaglutide and tirzepatide RCTs document injection cadence, dose escalation, GI adverse events, and discontinuation that bound patient burden.
evidence_trace.limitations: Trial burden does not fully capture real-world cost/access friction or long-term regain risk after stopping.

citation.title: Once-weekly semaglutide in adults with overweight or obesity

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Weight-loss efficacy is RCT-backed; cardiovascular outcome value is high only in close-match established-CVD populations and model-translated in primary prevention.

applies_when.interpretation: Use after office, home, or ambulatory readings support persistent stage-2 hypertension and secondary causes or sequencing issues are considered.

c1_prevention.basis: Stroke, heart failure, coronary, kidney, and dementia-risk reduction from sustained BP lowering.

c2_capacity.basis: Small lived-quality benefit through lower symptom burden and safer training clearance when BP is controlled.

c3_resilience.basis: Lower vulnerability during acute illness or physiologic stress.

burden_qaly.basis: Side effects, dose titration, labs, orthostasis risk, and monitoring.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Strong BP-event evidence, but patient-specific QALY depends on confirmed average BP, competing drivers such as sleep apnea, and medication tolerance.
overlap_rules: Do not score from one isolated BP reading.
If sleep apnea treatment materially lowers BP, reconcile shared BP-mediated prevention value.
Do not let BP treatment replace upstream sleep or weight drivers when those are active.

evidence_trace.transformation: SPRINT provides RCT evidence that intensive BP treatment reduces major cardiovascular events and mortality in eligible high-risk nondiabetic adults.
evidence_trace.limitations: Excludes diabetes and stroke; routine-office BP measurement differs from SPRINT protocol; patient-specific absolute benefit depends on baseline risk.

citation.title: A randomized trial of intensive versus standard blood-pressure control

evidence_trace.transformation: Confirmed hypertension treatment can improve safety for activity clearance and reduce symptom/sequelae burden.
evidence_trace.limitations: C2 lived-quality benefit is small and not the primary endpoint in BP trials.

citation.title: USPSTF hypertension in adults: screening

evidence_trace.transformation: SPRINT event and mortality reductions support lower physiologic vulnerability under cardiovascular stress in eligible patients.
evidence_trace.limitations: C3 is an interpretation of event reduction/reserve, not a separately measured utility endpoint.

citation.title: Final report of a trial of intensive versus standard blood-pressure control

evidence_trace.transformation: SPRINT reports treatment-related adverse events and monitoring burden, including hypotension, syncope, electrolyte abnormalities, and AKI.
evidence_trace.limitations: Burden varies by drug class, frailty, BP target, and monitoring context.

citation.title: A randomized trial of intensive versus standard blood-pressure control

evidence_axis.basis_summary: BP treatment benefit and harms are RCT/guideline supported for confirmed hypertension, with patient-specific absolute benefit determined by baseline risk and tolerance.

applies_when.interpretation: Use when preserving or building lean mass, strength, bone loading, and functional reserve is a real patient-specific lever.

c1_prevention.basis: Falls, frailty, metabolic health, and bone-risk prevention.

c2_capacity.basis: Independence, body ease, physical readiness, strength, and training tolerance.

c3_resilience.basis: Reserve for illness, injury, surgery, and later-life function.

burden_qaly.basis: Time, soreness, injury risk, coaching burden, and behavior friction.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Moderate. Functional benefit is clinically credible, but realized value depends on progression, adherence, coaching quality, and baseline reserve.
overlap_rules: If the broad physical_activity bundle is active, allocate shared capacity value rather than summing both totals.
When paired with GLP-1/GIP therapy, credit lean-mass preservation once.
Do not substitute generic activity minutes for progressive resistance work.

evidence_trace.transformation: Muscle-strengthening cohort meta-analysis supports modest prevention association for mortality, CVD, cancer, and diabetes.
evidence_trace.limitations: Mostly observational; supervised resistance training RCTs are stronger for function than hard outcomes.

citation.title: Muscle-strengthening activities and risk of mortality

evidence_trace.transformation: Resistance-training RCT meta-analyses support improved strength, lean mass, balance, and function; supervision improves functional outcomes in older adults.
evidence_trace.limitations: Functional endpoints require utility translation and vary by baseline age, frailty, and program quality.

citation.title: Supervised versus unsupervised training programs for older adults

evidence_trace.transformation: Exercise and supervised balance/resistance programs reduce fall risk and improve reserve-relevant function, especially in older adults.
evidence_trace.limitations: Younger-patient resilience effect remains extrapolated from older/fall-risk data.

citation.title: Exercise for preventing falls in older people living in the community

evidence_trace.transformation: D4 treats supervised training friction as sustained per-year burden, including time, soreness, injury risk, and coaching burden.
evidence_trace.limitations: No parameter-specific utility decrement source attached yet.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Resistance training has strong function evidence and supportive observational prevention evidence, but hard-outcome QALY magnitude is translated.

applies_when.interpretation: Required preventive care gap. Route as must-do even when ranking and patient-QALY display are separate.

c1_prevention.basis: Colorectal cancer prevention or earlier detection through guideline-concordant screening.

c2_capacity.basis: Capacity benefit is not the primary mechanism.

c3_resilience.basis: Earlier detection improves treatment burden and survivorship if cancer is present.

burden_qaly.basis: Preparation, procedure or stool-test burden, false positives, complication risk, and follow-up cascade.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Strong guideline basis. Patient-specific QALY varies by modality, findings, family history, and prior screening status.
overlap_rules: Required care gap status is independent of optional action ranking.
Do not compare as an optional wellness intervention when the patient is out of routine screening compliance.
Avoid double counting with separate colonoscopy-polypectomy benefit if modeled later.

evidence_trace.transformation: USPSTF recommends colorectal screening for eligible adults and identifies net benefit by age band and modality.
evidence_trace.limitations: Average-risk guidance; QALY magnitude depends on age, modality, screening history, and follow-up colonoscopy completion.

citation.title: USPSTF colorectal cancer screening recommendation

evidence_trace.transformation: USPSTF decision modeling estimates life-years gained and complications across screening strategies, supporting earlier-detection survivorship value.
evidence_trace.limitations: Model reports life-years and procedures more directly than patient QALY.

citation.title: USPSTF colorectal cancer screening decision modeling

evidence_trace.transformation: USPSTF modeling and recommendation statements bound burden by test modality, colonoscopy load, false positives, and complications.
evidence_trace.limitations: Burden differs substantially between FIT, sDNA-FIT, colonoscopy, CT colonography, and follow-up cascades.

citation.title: USPSTF colorectal cancer screening decision modeling

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Guideline-backed screening with decision-model support for eligible adults; harms and modality burden are well characterized.

applies_when.interpretation: Required genetics guidance and condition-specific surveillance routing. Specific actions depend on sex, family history, variant context, and current guidelines.

c1_prevention.basis: Earlier risk-appropriate surveillance, cascade testing, and prevention discussions for ATM-associated cancers.

c2_capacity.basis: Small value from clearer risk understanding and avoided uncertainty when counseling is done well.

c3_resilience.basis: Earlier detection or prevention planning can reduce treatment burden if disease occurs.

burden_qaly.basis: Counseling time, anxiety, cascade-testing burden, surveillance burden, and possible false reassurance or over-escalation.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Moderate for routing obligation, wider for QALY because surveillance value depends on variant, sex, family history, and guideline-specific eligibility.
overlap_rules: Surface as required genetics guidance when triggered, even if QALY remains uncertain.
Do not collapse all ATM-associated screening into one undifferentiated intervention once guideline modules exist.
Do not double count with organ-specific screening actions added later.

evidence_trace.transformation: NCCN/GeneReviews management implies counseling, cascade testing, surveillance cadence, and anxiety burden.
evidence_trace.limitations: Professional NCCN details are context-dependent and may require login; burden magnitude is not a direct utility estimate.

citation.title: NCCN Genetic/Familial High-Risk Assessment: Breast, Ovarian, Pancreatic, and Prostate

evidence_trace.transformation: ATM pathogenic variants alter breast, pancreatic, and prostate risk management, enabling risk-appropriate surveillance and counseling.
evidence_trace.limitations: Cancer-risk and management depend on sex, age, variant, and family history; QALY magnitude requires separate modeling.

citation.title: NCCN Genetic/Familial High-Risk Assessment: Breast, Ovarian, Pancreatic, and Prostate

evidence_trace.transformation: Genetic counseling can reduce uncertainty and align preventive planning, supporting a small C2 channel.
evidence_trace.limitations: Risk-clarity value is preference-sensitive and can be negative if anxiety dominates.

citation.title: GeneReviews: ATM-related cancer predisposition

evidence_trace.transformation: ATM penetrance evidence supports materially altered cancer-risk category, making earlier detection/planning a resilience pathway.
evidence_trace.limitations: Penetrance is ascertainment-, variant-, and family-history-dependent; QALY remains model-derived.

citation.title: Risk of pancreatic cancer among individuals with pathogenic variants in ATM

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Guideline-backed genetics management and penetrance evidence support risk routing, but QALY magnitude depends on variant, sex, family history, and modeled surveillance value.

applies_when.interpretation: Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes baseline_event_risk * relative_risk_reduction * event_value_qaly from derivation_inputs. These bands mirror the runner inputs for audit display.

c2_capacity.basis: No direct felt-quality channel credited for this hard-outcome renal drug action.

c3_resilience.basis: No separate resilience channel credited; residual-event tolerance is not independently parameterized.

burden_qaly.basis: Daily oral SGLT2 inhibitor therapy, genital/volume adverse-effect monitoring, renal function checks, and sick-day/periprocedural holds. Burden is per year and integrated over the 3-year evidence horizon for this valuation.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: progression to kidney failure / sustained >=50% eGFR loss / sustained low eGFR / death from kidney failure (diabetes-inclusive CKD)

basis.baseline_event_risk: Albuminuric CKD cardiorenal-trial composite-event baseline risk, calibrated as a 3-year treated-eligible phenotype risk band from large placebo-controlled SGLT2i CKD/cardiorenal trials and meta-analysis control arms.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Versioned clinical effect from evidence_trace effect.relative_risk_reduction.
basis.event_value_qaly: Composite kidney-failure/eGFR-loss/death event value uses the internal D2 event-value method: lower bound for nonfatal progression delay, central value for kidney-failure/death composite morbidity, upper bound for kidney failure or renal death with durable quality and survival loss.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Matches the approximate randomized evidence follow-up horizon rather than extrapolating drug burden or benefit to lifetime.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly.central * sustained_horizon_years

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands combine baseline-event-risk uncertainty, clinical effect precision, internal event-value band, and therapy-burden band. No C2 or C3 value is credited.
overlap_rules: Do not double count the same renal/CV outcome reduction across adjacent drug classes in the same treatment stack.
Combination therapy must be represented by an explicit bundle or incremental effect model, not summed independent action values.

citation.source_text: Nuffield Department of Population Health Renal Studies Group; SGLT2 inhibitor Meta-Analysis Cardio-Renal Trialists' Consortium (SMART-C) (Staplin N, Haynes R, Mayne KJ, et al.). Impact of diabetes on the effects of sodium glucose co-transporter-2 inhibitors on kidney outcomes: collaborative meta-analysis of large placebo-controlled trials. Lancet. 2022 Nov 19;400(10365):1788-1801. https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lancet/article/PIIS0140-6736(22)02074-8/fulltext

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert RR 0.63 into relative risk reduction 0.37; runner will apply to patient-specific baseline risk and event value.
evidence_trace.limitations: Effect is population-level; patient value depends on eligibility, baseline risk, competing risks, sustained adherence, and overlap with other therapies.

citation.source_text: Nuffield Department of Population Health Renal Studies Group; SGLT2 inhibitor Meta-Analysis Cardio-Renal Trialists' Consortium (SMART-C) (Staplin N, Haynes R, Mayne KJ, et al.). Impact of diabetes on the effects of sodium glucose co-transporter-2 inhibitors on kidney outcomes: collaborative meta-analysis of large placebo-controlled trials. Lancet. 2022 Nov 19;400(10365):1788-1801. https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lancet/article/PIIS0140-6736(22)02074-8/fulltext

evidence_trace.transformation: Albuminuric CKD cardiorenal-trial composite-event baseline risk, calibrated as a 3-year treated-eligible phenotype risk band from large placebo-controlled SGLT2i CKD/cardiorenal trials and meta-analysis control arms.
evidence_trace.limitations: Trial-arm baseline risk is an explicit phenotype binding, not a live individualized CKD risk model. Replace with patient-specific CKD progression risk when that model is validated.

evidence_trace.transformation: Composite kidney-failure/eGFR-loss/death event value uses the internal D2 event-value method: lower bound for nonfatal progression delay, central value for kidney-failure/death composite morbidity, upper bound for kidney failure or renal death with durable quality and survival loss.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal patient-value model, not a direct trial-measured utility result; kept medium-capped through model_translated_value_cap.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by the event_or_curve_shift_action runner from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and the explicit horizon.
evidence_trace.limitations: Does not add capacity/resilience value and does not model incremental combination effects inside a drug stack.

evidence_trace.transformation: Daily oral SGLT2 inhibitor therapy, genital/volume adverse-effect monitoring, renal function checks, and sick-day/periprocedural holds. Burden is per year and integrated over the 3-year evidence horizon for this valuation.
evidence_trace.limitations: Burden magnitude is internally calibrated by therapy class and should be upgraded with direct patient time, adverse-event, and preference data.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly at declared cadence

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands combine trial-phenotype baseline-event-risk uncertainty, relative-effect precision, internal event-value translation, medication burden cadence, eligibility gates, and overlap constraints. C2 and C3 are honest zeroes.
overlap_rules: Member of secondary_stroke_prevention, recurrent_mi_prevention, insulin_resistance, diabetes_prevention, medication_harm, heart_failure_harm, fracture_harm, hospitalization, disability, and mortality overlap families; do not sum with antiplatelet therapy, statins, BP control, smoking cessation, GLP-1/SGLT2, metformin, lifestyle, sleep apnea treatment, carotid intervention, or rehab records unless endpoint slices are explicitly partitioned.
HF, edema, weight, and fracture harms are handled as burden/adverse-effect vigilance here; if endpoint-specific harm event models are later added, reduce this burden slice to avoid double counting.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert IRIS HR 0.76 into central relative_risk_reduction 0.24 for recurrent fatal or nonfatal stroke or MI in non-diabetic insulin-resistant patients after ischemic stroke or TIA; runner applies this to explicit baseline_event_risk and event_value_qaly.
evidence_trace.limitations: Eligibility is narrow and net value is sensitive to edema, weight gain, heart failure, fracture, adherence, and discontinuation harms.

citation.source_text: Kernan WN, Viscoli CM, Furie KL, et al. Pioglitazone after Ischemic Stroke or Transient Ischemic Attack. N Engl J Med. 2016;374(14):1321-1331.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use IRIS placebo-arm primary recurrent stroke-or-MI risk over median 4.8 years as explicit baseline_event_risk, centered at 0.118 and widened to 0.09-0.15.
evidence_trace.limitations: Trial baseline risk is a narrow secondary-prevention phenotype binding, not a live individualized recurrent stroke/MI model.

basis.baseline_event_risk: Three-year incident diabetes risk band for overweight/obese prediabetic DPP-like phenotype, anchored to DPP-like entry criteria rather than low-risk dysglycemia.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Versioned clinical effect from evidence_trace effect.relative_risk_reduction.
basis.event_value_qaly: Incident diabetes event value uses internal downstream diabetes-complication valuation: lower bound for diagnosis/monitoring burden, central for long-run microvascular/metabolic burden, high for higher-risk complications.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Evidence/decision horizon used by the runner for burden integration and near-term intrinsic-value display.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly.central * sustained_horizon_years for per_year burden, or minus burden_qaly.central for one_time burden

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands combine trial-phenotype baseline-event risk, relative-effect uncertainty, internal event-value band, burden band, phenotype eligibility, and overlap constraints. No C2/C3 value is included.
overlap_rules: Member of weight_loss_lifestyle overlap family; do not add independently to ir_lifestyle_prevention, direct_weight_remission, physical_activity_metabolic, or med_diet_metabolic unless endpoint and mechanism slices are partitioned.
Do not double count with incretin_prevention_obesity or metabolic_surgery; use one dominant weight-loss pathway or explicit incremental sequence model.
If physical activity or Mediterranean diet records are also selected, count shared coaching, diet, and exercise burden once and avoid duplicate C2 capacity credit.

citation.source_text: Hamman RF, Wing RR, Edelstein SL, et al. Effect of weight loss with lifestyle intervention on risk of diabetes. Diabetes Care. 2006;29(9):2102-2107.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use the batch-007 conservative HR 0.76 whole-program scalar as relative risk reduction 0.24; bands reflect smaller or larger achieved weight loss in DPP-like implementation.
evidence_trace.limitations: Population-level relative effect; patient value depends on eligibility, baseline risk, persistence/durability, contraindications, adverse effects, and overlap with competing interventions.

evidence_trace.transformation: Three-year incident diabetes risk band for overweight/obese prediabetic DPP-like phenotype, anchored to DPP-like entry criteria rather than low-risk dysglycemia.
evidence_trace.limitations: This is explicit trial-phenotype or eligible-phenotype binding, not a fully individualized live risk model. Replace with patient-specific risk when validated.

evidence_trace.transformation: Incident diabetes event value uses internal downstream diabetes-complication valuation: lower bound for diagnosis/monitoring burden, central for long-run microvascular/metabolic burden, high for higher-risk complications.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal patient-value model, not a direct trial-measured utility result; kept medium-capped through model_translated_value_cap.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by the event_or_curve_shift_action runner from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and explicit burden cadence.
evidence_trace.limitations: Does not add capacity/resilience value and does not model incremental combination effects inside an obesity, diabetes-prevention, or lifestyle stack.

evidence_trace.transformation: Intensive lifestyle-program burden is per year over 3 years and includes coaching visits, food tracking, exercise time, cost/access friction, and adherence burden.
evidence_trace.limitations: Burden magnitude is internally calibrated and should be upgraded with direct patient time, adverse-event, and preference data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: DPP-style evidence supports incident diabetes reduction, but this fully derived record credits only the C1 diabetes-prevention channel; dose-specific C2/C3 lifestyle value remains uncredited until a lifestyle multichannel runner is available.

applies_when.interpretation: Narrow map-eligible valuation for active tobacco use in an albuminuric CKD phenotype where structured cessation is expected to reduce CKD progression risk. This does not claim the full tobacco-cessation opportunity across cancer, COPD, or cardiovascular endpoints.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes baseline CKD-progression event risk times cessation-associated relative risk reduction times CKD-progression event_value_qaly. Cancer, COPD, cardiovascular, capacity, and resilience value are honest zeroes pending endpoint-specific runners.

c2_capacity.basis: No separate dyspnea, energy, taste, smell, or lived-quality channel is credited until sourced C2 utility inputs are added.

c3_resilience.basis: No separate surgical, infection, or recovery resilience channel is credited until residual-event severity and recovery inputs are sourced.

burden_qaly.basis: One front-loaded quit-attempt burden covering counseling, medication selection when appropriate, withdrawal symptoms, relapse-prevention follow-up, household exposure management, cost/access friction, and adverse effects. Ongoing maintenance burden is not separately charged in this narrow one-year quit-attempt valuation.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: CKD progression in active smokers with albuminuric CKD after structured smoking-cessation support

basis.baseline_event_risk: Three-year CKD-progression risk band for an albuminuric CKD phenotype, used as explicit baseline-risk binding rather than a general active-smoker population risk.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Convert the reviewed smoking-cessation CKD-progression association into central RRR 0.20 with broad bands for exposure intensity, abstinence durability, relapse, and residual confounding.
basis.event_value_qaly: CKD-progression event value uses D2_event_value_qaly_v1 for kidney-function decline and downstream morbidity only; tobacco-attributable cancer, COPD, and CVD event values are deliberately excluded.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Evidence horizon for CKD-progression valuation. Burden remains one_time because the audited burden is the front-loaded quit attempt, not a three-year adherence haircut.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly at declared cadence

cohort_projection.display_note: Narrow CKD-progression-only valuation; engine computes C1 from derivation_inputs and subtracts one-time quit-attempt burden.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands reflect CKD baseline risk, pack-year intensity, achieved abstinence, relapse, time-since-quit risk decay, event-value uncertainty, and front-loaded cessation burden. Non-CKD tobacco endpoints remain excluded, not zero-valued as a clinical claim.
overlap_rules: Narrow CKD-progression-only member of smoking_cessation. Do not add to broader tobacco cancer, COPD, cardiovascular, or mortality values unless endpoint slices are explicitly partitioned.
Do not double count CKD-progression value with SGLT2, RAAS, finerenone, BP, or dietary CKD records unless residual CKD risk is partitioned.
If LDCT screening or cancer-prevention records are selected, keep diagnostic VOI and primary-prevention endpoints separate from this CKD-only valuation.

citation.source_text: Lee S, Kang S, Joo YS, et al. Smoking, Smoking Cessation, and Progression of Chronic Kidney Disease: Results From KNOW-CKD Study. Nicotine Tob Res. 2021;23(1):92-98.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use central RRR 0.20 for CKD progression and widen to 0.08 to 0.32 because cessation durability, exposure intensity, and residual confounding are not patient-bound in this generic valuation.
evidence_trace.limitations: Cohort evidence and one CKD endpoint only; this record is not a full tobacco-cessation value across pulmonary, cancer, or cardiovascular endpoints.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind the record to an albuminuric CKD progression-risk phenotype with a three-year risk band rather than applying a general-population smoker baseline.
evidence_trace.limitations: Explicit phenotype binding, not a live individualized CKD progression model. Replace with patient-specific CKD risk when validated.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use CKD-progression-only D2 event value for decline in kidney function and downstream morbidity, excluding tobacco-attributable cancer, COPD, and CVD endpoints.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal patient-value model, medium-capped and endpoint-limited.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and explicit burden cadence.
evidence_trace.limitations: Only the CKD-progression C1 channel is credited; C2/C3 and other endpoints remain uncredited.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply a one-time quit-attempt burden for counseling, medication, withdrawal, relapse prevention, access, and household exposure management.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration; should be upgraded with direct patient time, withdrawal, adverse-effect, and preference data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Cessation has strong clinical rationale, but this map-eligible record is deliberately narrow: cohort-supported CKD-progression C1 value in active smokers with albuminuric CKD, medium-capped for model-translated event value and internal burden calibration.

applies_when.interpretation: Narrow map-eligible valuation for hazardous or heavy alcohol use where the patient matches a recurrent atrial-fibrillation risk phenotype and reduction or abstinence is clinically safe. It does not claim full liver, cancer, trauma, mood, sleep, or BP-mediated alcohol value.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes recurrent-AF baseline risk times alcohol-reduction relative risk reduction times recurrent-AF event_value_qaly. Non-AF alcohol endpoints and C2/C3 channels are honest zeroes pending endpoint-specific runners.

c2_capacity.basis: No separate sleep, mood, energy, GI, or role-function utility is credited until sourced C2 utility inputs are added.

c3_resilience.basis: No separate trauma, perioperative, infection, or residual-event resilience channel is credited until residual-event inputs are sourced.

burden_qaly.basis: One behavior-change initiation burden covering screening, brief intervention or treatment initiation, counseling, medication when appropriate, withdrawal-risk assessment, social friction, tracking, access/cost, and relapse planning.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: recurrent atrial fibrillation in regular drinkers with established AF after alcohol abstinence or substantial reduction

basis.baseline_event_risk: Six-month recurrent-AF risk band for a heavy-drinking established-AF phenotype, used as explicit phenotype binding rather than a general hazardous-drinking baseline.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Convert the reviewed abstinence-trial HR 0.70 into central RRR 0.30, with broad bands for achieved reduction, relapse, monitoring intensity, and patient phenotype.
basis.event_value_qaly: Recurrent-AF event value covers symptomatic recurrence, care utilization, and short-horizon quality decrement only; stroke, liver disease, cancer, trauma, sleep, and mood value are deliberately excluded.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Matches the six-month randomized recurrence horizon for this narrow AF endpoint.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly at declared cadence

cohort_projection.display_note: Narrow recurrent-AF-only valuation; engine computes C1 from derivation_inputs and subtracts one-time behavior-change initiation burden.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands reflect recurrent-AF baseline risk, baseline drinking pattern, achieved reduction or abstinence, relapse, AF monitoring intensity, short-horizon recurrent-AF event value, withdrawal safety, and behavior-change burden. Non-AF alcohol outcomes remain excluded.
overlap_rules: Narrow recurrent-AF-only member of alcohol_reduction. Do not add to stroke, BP, liver disease, cancer, trauma, sleep, or mood alcohol values unless endpoint slices are explicitly partitioned.
Do not double count AF or stroke value with rhythm-management, anticoagulation, BP, sleep apnea, or weight-loss records unless residual risk is partitioned.
C2 sleep, mood, and role-function gains must be assigned once across alcohol, sleep, depression, and weight interventions when sourced.

citation.source_text: Voskoboinik A, et al. Alcohol Abstinence in Drinkers with Atrial Fibrillation. N Engl J Med. 2020;382(1):20-28.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert HR 0.70 for AF recurrence into central RRR 0.30; widen bands for achieved alcohol reduction, relapse, AF monitoring, and phenotype mismatch.
evidence_trace.limitations: Strongest for recurrent AF in regular drinkers with established AF; not a full alcohol-attributable disease valuation.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind to a six-month recurrent-AF phenotype risk band instead of applying the AF trial effect to all hazardous drinkers.
evidence_trace.limitations: Explicit phenotype binding, not a live individualized AF-recurrence model. Requires clinical AF-history confirmation before recommendation.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use recurrent-AF-only event value for symptomatic recurrence and care utilization; exclude stroke, liver, cancer, trauma, mood, sleep, and BP-mediated pathways.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal short-horizon event-value model, medium-capped and endpoint-limited.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and explicit burden cadence.
evidence_trace.limitations: Only recurrent-AF prevention is credited; C2/C3 and other alcohol endpoints remain uncredited.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply one initiation burden for alcohol reduction or abstinence support, including counseling, withdrawal-risk assessment, social friction, tracking, access, and relapse planning.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration; must be upgraded for AUD severity, withdrawal risk, medication choice, and patient preference.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: RCT evidence supports alcohol abstinence for recurrent AF in regular drinkers, but this fully derived record is intentionally endpoint-limited and medium-capped for internal AF event-value and burden calibration.

applies_when.interpretation: Narrow map-eligible valuation for a SSaSS-like salt-substitution or sodium-potassium exposure intervention in elevated-BP or high-sodium phenotype. It does not claim the full diet, weight, exercise, CKD, or dementia lifestyle bundle value.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes baseline major cardiovascular/stroke event risk times achieved salt-substitution relative risk reduction times event_value_qaly. Lifestyle capacity, renal resilience, and dementia value are honest zeroes pending separate runners.

c2_capacity.basis: No separate energy, function, diet-quality, or weight-related capacity channel is credited until a sourced lifestyle C2 utility anchor is implemented and de-duplicated.

c3_resilience.basis: No separate residual stroke-severity, renal-reserve, or recovery resilience channel is credited until residual-event inputs are sourced.

burden_qaly.basis: Per-year burden for salt substitution or focused sodium-potassium exposure change, including taste adaptation, household cooking friction, purchase/access, home BP checks during titration, coaching, and potassium-safety review when CKD or RAAS therapy is present.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: major cardiovascular events and stroke in a high-risk elevated-BP or high-sodium phenotype after salt substitution or focused sodium-potassium exposure change

basis.baseline_event_risk: SSaSS-like high-risk major cardiovascular/stroke event-risk band over 4.7 years, used as explicit phenotype binding rather than applying salt substitution to low-risk normotensive patients.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Convert SSaSS major cardiovascular event RR about 0.87 into central RRR 0.13; widen bands for achieved BP/sodium-potassium change, baseline risk, adherence, and safety context.
basis.event_value_qaly: Major cardiovascular/stroke event value uses D2_event_value_qaly_v1 for stroke, acute coronary syndrome, cardiovascular death, and serious morbidity, without adding capacity or resilience credit.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Matches the randomized salt-substitution evidence horizon for benefit and per-year burden integration.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly at declared cadence

cohort_projection.display_note: Narrow salt-substitution CVD/stroke valuation; engine computes C1 from derivation_inputs and subtracts per-year burden over the evidence horizon.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands reflect baseline BP/CVD risk, achieved systolic BP or sodium-potassium exposure change, CKD and hyperkalemia safety context, adherence durability, major-event value uncertainty, and per-year lifestyle burden. Non-BP lifestyle capacity, weight, CKD, and dementia value remain excluded.
overlap_rules: Narrow salt-substitution or sodium-potassium CVD/stroke member of bp_lifestyle. Do not add to DASH, weight loss, physical activity, dietary sodium/protein CKD, or antihypertensive records without endpoint and achieved-BP partitioning.

Do not double count BP-mediated CVD, stroke, CKD, or dementia value with pharmacologic BP lowering, intensive BP dementia records, CKD RAAS/SGLT2 pathways, or ASCVD medication records unless residual-risk pathways are explicit.
If potassium-enriched salt substitute is selected in CKD or RAAS-treated patients, safety burden and hyperkalemia risk must be represented before value is scored.

citation.source_text: Neal B, Wu Y, Feng X, et al. Effect of Salt Substitution on Cardiovascular Events and Death. N Engl J Med. 2021;385(12):1067-1077.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert RR 0.87 for major cardiovascular events into central RRR 0.13; widen bands for achieved BP change, sodium-potassium exposure, population match, and safety context.
evidence_trace.limitations: SSaSS was high-risk and context-specific; this record is a narrow salt-substitution CVD/stroke valuation, not a general lifestyle bundle.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind to a high-risk elevated-BP or high-sodium phenotype with a 4.7-year major-event risk band instead of applying salt substitution uniformly.
evidence_trace.limitations: Explicit phenotype binding, not a live individualized BP/CVD/stroke model. Replace with patient-specific risk when validated.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use major cardiovascular/stroke event value for stroke, acute coronary syndrome, cardiovascular death, and serious morbidity, excluding C2 diet quality, renal resilience, and dementia endpoints.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal composite event-value model, medium-capped and endpoint-limited.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and explicit burden cadence.
evidence_trace.limitations: Only CVD/stroke prevention is credited; C2/C3 and broader lifestyle overlap remain uncredited.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply per-year salt-substitution burden over 4.7 years, including taste adaptation, household cooking friction, access, home BP checks, coaching, and potassium-safety review.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration; should be upgraded with patient time, acceptability, adverse-event, and food-access data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Randomized hard-outcome evidence supports a narrow salt-substitution CVD/stroke valuation, but this record remains medium-capped for model-translated composite event value, phenotype binding, safety context, and internal burden calibration.

applies_when.interpretation: Narrow map-eligible valuation for metformin prevention only when a DPP-like prediabetes/high-transition-risk phenotype is present and metformin is clinically appropriate after kidney function, B12 status, pregnancy context, GI tolerance, and competing lifestyle or incretin prevention are checked.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes incident-diabetes baseline_event_risk times metformin relative_risk_reduction times incident-diabetes event_value_qaly. C2/C3 are honest zeroes until separate sourced capacity or residual-severity runners exist.

c2_capacity.basis: No separate weight, energy, monitoring-relief, or felt-quality capacity channel is credited; those pathways must be sourced and de-duplicated from lifestyle and incretin records before scoring.

c3_resilience.basis: No separate residual diabetes-severity or complication-resilience channel is credited until residual-event severity inputs are sourced separately from incident-diabetes prevention.

burden_qaly.basis: Daily metformin burden is per year over the DPP evidence horizon and includes titration, GI symptoms, pill burden, renal-function review, B12 monitoring, contraindication checks, cost/access friction, and discontinuation review.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: incident type-2 diabetes delayed or prevented in a DPP-like prediabetes/high-transition-risk phenotype treated with tolerated metformin

basis.baseline_event_risk: Three-year incident diabetes risk band for DPP-like prediabetes phenotype, anchored to the placebo-group transition risk in the Diabetes Prevention Program and narrowed to patients with comparable dysglycemia rather than all low-risk prediabetes.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Convert DPP metformin HR about 0.69 into central RRR 0.31; widen bands for glycemic phenotype, BMI/age/gestational-diabetes subgroup, adherence, tolerance, and persistence.
basis.event_value_qaly: Incident diabetes event value uses the existing internal downstream diabetes-complication valuation shared with DPP/incretin prevention, but credits only incident-diabetes prevention; separate C2 weight/energy and downstream complication pathways remain excluded unless explicitly modeled.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Uses the DPP randomized evidence horizon for benefit and per-year burden integration.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly.central * sustained_horizon_years for per_year burden, or minus burden_qaly.central for one_time burden

cohort_projection.display_note: Narrow incident-diabetes-prevention valuation; engine computes C1 from derivation_inputs and subtracts per-year medication burden over the DPP evidence horizon.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands reflect baseline diabetes-transition risk, A1c/fasting glucose/OGTT phenotype, BMI, age, gestational-diabetes history, adherence and GI intolerance, renal/B12 monitoring, incident-diabetes event-value uncertainty, and overlap with lifestyle, weight-loss, and incretin prevention. C2/C3 are intentionally uncredited.
overlap_rules: Member of diabetes_prevention overlap family; do not double count incident-diabetes prevention with dpp_lifestyle, incretin_prevention_obesity, ir_lifestyle_prevention, or other weight-loss/metabolic prevention records.
If physical activity, diet, weight loss, or incretin therapy is also selected, partition incident-diabetes prevention and shared behavior-support burden before scoring.
Do not credit downstream CKD, CVD, retinopathy, neuropathy, weight, energy, or monitoring-relief value unless mediated through a single incident-diabetes and complication-trajectory model.

citation.source_text: Knowler WC, Barrett-Connor E, Fowler SE, Hamman RF, Lachin JM, Walker EA, Nathan DM; Diabetes Prevention Program Research Group. Reduction in the incidence of type 2 diabetes with lifestyle intervention or metformin. N Engl J Med. 2002;346(6):393-403.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert DPP metformin HR about 0.69 into central RRR 0.31 for incident type-2 diabetes; use wider low/high bands for phenotype, adherence, tolerance, and persistence uncertainty.
evidence_trace.limitations: RCT endpoint is incident diabetes, but patient QALY is still model-translated through an internal incident-diabetes event value and excludes unmodeled C2/C3 benefits.

citation.source_text: DPP placebo-group diabetes incidence supplies the DPP-like prediabetes baseline-risk anchor.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use a three-year incident-diabetes risk band for DPP-like prediabetes/high-transition-risk phenotype rather than applying metformin to all mild dysglycemia.
evidence_trace.limitations: Explicit phenotype binding, not a live individualized diabetes-transition model; replace with patient-specific risk when validated.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use the D2 incident-diabetes event-value band shared with existing DPP/incretin prevention records, limited to diagnosis, treatment, monitoring, and downstream metabolic complication burden.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal event-value model, medium-capped and not a full diabetes complication microsimulation.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and explicit burden cadence.
evidence_trace.limitations: Only incident-diabetes prevention is credited; C2/C3 and broader downstream disease pathways remain uncredited.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply per-year metformin burden over the DPP evidence horizon, including daily use, titration, GI effects, renal and B12 monitoring, contraindication checks, and discontinuation review.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration; should be upgraded with patient-specific tolerance, dose, monitoring frequency, and preference data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: RCT evidence directly supports incident-diabetes prevention in DPP-like prediabetes, but patient QALY remains medium-capped because the record uses model-translated incident-diabetes event value, phenotype-bound baseline risk, adherence/tolerance assumptions, and internal medication-burden calibration.

applies_when.interpretation: Applies only to a confirmed or strongly suspected idiopathic normal-pressure hydrocephalus phenotype with gait-predominant impairment, ventriculomegaly, mimics excluded, acceptable surgical risk, and response to temporary CSF drainage or equivalent shunt-candidacy evidence. This valuation is not for nonspecific gait decline.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes shunt-responsive NPH baseline gait/fall/dependency event risk times relative risk reduction times a D2 gait-disability/fall-dependency event_value_qaly. C2 gait capacity is not separately credited to avoid double counting.

c2_capacity.basis: Honest zero in this promoted record. Mobility and independence value is carried inside the C1 gait/fall/dependency event-value object until a separate sourced utility anchor is available.

c3_resilience.basis: Honest zero. Residual fall severity, shunt durability, revision, and recovery effects are not independently parameterized.

burden_qaly.basis: One-time NPH shunt pathway burden covering diagnostic workup and temporary drainage trial, shunt surgery, hospitalization/recovery, valve adjustments, surveillance, complications, infection, malfunction, and revision risk.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: gait-predominant disability, injurious falls, continence-related functional dependence, and loss of independence in shunt-responsive idiopathic normal-pressure hydrocephalus

basis.baseline_event_risk: Explicit eligible-phenotype binding for shunt-candidate NPH with gait impairment and drainage-response evidence over a near-term post-shunt decision horizon; not a general older-adult fall-risk baseline.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Convert the reviewed PENS shunt trial RR 0.53 into central RRR 0.47 and widen for diagnostic certainty, drainage response, frailty, surgical risk, and shunt durability.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal D2 event value for gait/fall/dependency composite, covering disability and independence loss without adding a separate C2 utility channel.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: One-year near-term post-procedure functional horizon; longer shunt durability and revision effects are not extrapolated.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly at declared cadence

cohort_projection.display_note: Narrow shunt-responsive NPH gait/fall/dependency valuation; engine computes C1 from derivation_inputs and subtracts one-time procedure burden.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands reflect diagnostic certainty, drainage-response prediction, baseline gait/fall/dependency risk, surgical and revision risk, shunt durability, internal event-value mapping, and one-time procedure burden. C2 and C3 are honest zeroes.
overlap_rules: Do not double count the same gait, fall, continence, dependency, or independence benefit under fall-prevention, physical therapy, exercise, cognitive, or urinary-symptom records.
Separate NPH diagnostic workup VOI from the direct value of completed shunting in an eligible responder phenotype.
If a mobility or fall-prevention plan is also selected, assign shared fall and independence value once or require an explicit incremental model.

citation.source_text: Luciano MG, Williams MA, Hamilton MG, et al. A Randomized Trial of Shunting for Idiopathic Normal-Pressure Hydrocephalus (PENS trial). N Engl J Med. 2025;393(22):2198-2209.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert RR 0.53 to central RRR 0.47 for eligible shunt-responsive NPH; widen bands for selection, response, durability, and surgical-risk heterogeneity.
evidence_trace.limitations: Applies only to appropriately selected NPH, not nonspecific gait decline or advanced nonresponsive dementia.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind to confirmed gait-predominant, shunt-candidate NPH over a one-year functional/fall/dependency horizon.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal phenotype baseline until replaced by patient-specific gait speed, falls, continence, frailty, and drainage-response model.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use a gait/fall/dependency event-value object and keep separate C2 mobility utility at zero to avoid duplicate value.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal event-value model; utility mapping for gait and independence remains an upgrade.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and one-time burden cadence.
evidence_trace.limitations: No independent C2/C3 value is credited.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply one-time shunt-pathway burden after C1 value, including workup, surgery, recovery, surveillance, complications, and revision risk.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration pending patient-specific surgical-risk and revision-rate data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Randomized NPH shunt evidence supports a narrow gait/fall/dependency event valuation in a responder-gated phenotype, but model-translated event value, internal baseline-risk binding, durability, and procedure burden keep confidence medium.

applies_when.interpretation: Applies only when vision-impairing cataract is present and cataract extraction is clinically appropriate; visual acuity/contrast limitation, functional impairment, ocular comorbidity, first-eye or second-eye status, cognitive baseline, and surgical risk must be checked.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes baseline_event_risk times relative risk reduction times event_value_qaly for this narrow dementia/event endpoint. observational cataract-dementia association is causally guarded; direct visual C2 benefit is not credited without a sourced visual-utility anchor.

c2_capacity.basis: Honest zero. No separate capacity or felt-quality channel is credited until sourced C2 utility inputs are available and de-duplicated from C1.

c3_resilience.basis: Honest zero. Residual-event severity, recovery, or reserve effects are not independently parameterized.

burden_qaly.basis: One-time intervention burden at declared cadence, including eligibility review, counseling, scheduling, adherence friction, short-term adverse effects or procedure/training burden, and follow-up. Burden is subtracted after event value, not used as an adherence haircut.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: incident all-cause dementia in older adults with vision-impairing cataract after cataract extraction

basis.baseline_event_risk: Explicit phenotype-bound baseline event-risk band over the evidence horizon for the matched finding, not a general-population default.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Reviewed effect converted to RRR and causally/precision guarded with wide bands for phenotype match, adherence, and residual confounding.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal D2 event-value object for dementia/function event burden; no separate C2/C3 value is added.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Matches the evidence horizon for benefit display; burden remains one_time for the intervention course or procedure.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly at declared cadence

cohort_projection.display_note: Narrow C1-only valuation; engine computes from derivation_inputs and subtracts one-time burden.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands reflect baseline-risk uncertainty, relative-effect uncertainty, internal event-value translation, phenotype gating, adherence or procedure completion, overlap constraints, and burden. C2 and C3 are honest zeroes.
overlap_rules: Do not double count the same dementia, cognition, function, falls, sensory, pain, or independence value with adjacent neuro, sensory, vaccine, lifestyle, mood, sleep, or multidomain records.
If the action is embedded in a broader preventive or cognitive program, shared coaching, scheduling, procedure, technology, and follow-up burden must be assigned once.
Any direct capacity benefit requires a separate sourced C2 utility anchor and explicit endpoint partition before it can be added.

citation.source_text: Lee CS, Gibbons LE, Lee AY, et al. Association Between Cataract Extraction and Development of Dementia. JAMA Intern Med. 2022;182(2):134-141.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert the reviewed effect estimate to a guarded RRR band for the narrow endpoint and matched phenotype.
evidence_trace.limitations: Observational cataract-dementia association is causally guarded; direct visual c2 benefit is not credited without a sourced visual-utility anchor.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use explicit matched-phenotype baseline event-risk bands over the stated horizon before applying the relative effect.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal baseline-risk binding pending live individualized dementia/function model integration.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Observational cataract-dementia association is causally guarded; direct visual c2 benefit is not credited without a sourced visual-utility anchor, so the record is fully runner-computed but low-confidence and endpoint-limited.

applies_when.interpretation: Applies only when age, immune status, vaccine history, contraindications, and preventive-care context make recombinant zoster vaccination clinically appropriate. Dementia-risk phenotype stratifies the valuation but does not replace routine vaccine eligibility.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes baseline_event_risk times relative risk reduction times event_value_qaly for this narrow dementia/event endpoint. natural-experiment dementia association is causally guarded; established zoster and PHN prevention are deliberately excluded pending endpoint-specific source upgrade.

c2_capacity.basis: Honest zero. No separate capacity or felt-quality channel is credited until sourced C2 utility inputs are available and de-duplicated from C1.

c3_resilience.basis: Honest zero. Residual-event severity, recovery, or reserve effects are not independently parameterized.

burden_qaly.basis: One-time intervention burden at declared cadence, including eligibility review, counseling, scheduling, adherence friction, short-term adverse effects or procedure/training burden, and follow-up. Burden is subtracted after event value, not used as an adherence haircut.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: incident dementia after clinically appropriate zoster vaccination, with ordinary zoster and PHN value excluded until separately sourced

basis.baseline_event_risk: Explicit phenotype-bound baseline event-risk band over the evidence horizon for the matched finding, not a general-population default.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Reviewed effect converted to RRR and causally/precision guarded with wide bands for phenotype match, adherence, and residual confounding.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal D2 event-value object for dementia/function event burden; no separate C2/C3 value is added.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Matches the evidence horizon for benefit display; burden remains one_time for the intervention course or procedure.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly at declared cadence

cohort_projection.display_note: Narrow C1-only valuation; engine computes from derivation_inputs and subtracts one-time burden.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands reflect baseline-risk uncertainty, relative-effect uncertainty, internal event-value translation, phenotype gating, adherence or procedure completion, overlap constraints, and burden. C2 and C3 are honest zeroes.
overlap_rules: Do not double count the same dementia, cognition, function, falls, sensory, pain, or independence value with adjacent neuro, sensory, vaccine, lifestyle, mood, sleep, or multidomain records.
If the action is embedded in a broader preventive or cognitive program, shared coaching, scheduling, procedure, technology, and follow-up burden must be assigned once.
Any direct capacity benefit requires a separate sourced C2 utility anchor and explicit endpoint partition before it can be added.

citation.source_text: Eyting M, Xie M, Michalik F, Hess S, Chung S, Geldsetzer P. A natural experiment on the effect of herpes zoster vaccination on dementia. Nature. 2025;641(8062):438-446.

applies_when.interpretation: Applies only to chronic coronary disease or recent MI patients with residual inflammatory risk where low-dose colchicine is clinically appropriate after standard lipid, BP, antithrombotic, smoking, and diabetes risk management are addressed. Baseline risk is bound to the CAD residual-inflammation trial phenotype, not to a general elevated-hs-CRP population.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes baseline_event_risk * relative_risk_reduction * event_value_qaly from derivation_inputs for a narrow CAD residual-inflammation MACE endpoint.

c2_capacity.basis: No direct day-to-day capacity benefit is credited for low-dose colchicine absent a sourced symptom utility anchor.

c3_resilience.basis: No separate recovery or residual-event-severity benefit is credited beyond prevented MACE.

burden_qaly.basis: Daily low-dose oral therapy with renal/hepatic review, interaction checks, GI intolerance monitoring, myopathy and cytopenia vigilance, infection-signal review, and periodic reassessment. Burden is per year and integrated over the 2.5-year evidence horizon.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: major adverse cardiovascular events in chronic or post-MI coronary artery disease with residual inflammatory risk

basis.baseline_event_risk: Matched CAD/post-MI residual-inflammation trial-phenotype MACE risk over approximately 2.5 years; this is an explicit phenotype binding and not a population-wide hs-CRP risk model.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Central value preserves the COLCOT low-dose colchicine HR 0.77 as RRR 0.23, banded for effect transportability and supported directionally by LoDoCo2 chronic coronary disease evidence.
basis.event_value_qaly: Composite MACE event value uses the internal D2 event-value method for CV death, nonfatal MI, ischemic stroke, and urgent revascularization, with no additional C2/C3 credit.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Approximate randomized evidence horizon used to integrate chronic medication burden without lifetime extrapolation.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly.central * sustained_horizon_years

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands combine matched CAD baseline MACE risk, relative-effect precision and transportability, internal MACE event-value translation, daily medication burden, GI intolerance, renal/hepatic constraints, drug interactions, infection/myopathy signals, and overlap with standard ASCVD prevention.
overlap_rules: Do not double count the same MACE prevention with canakinumab_il1b, standard lipid therapy, BP therapy, antithrombotic therapy, smoking cessation, physical activity, diet, or diabetes therapy unless endpoint slices are explicitly partitioned.
hs-CRP or inflammation testing VOI is separate from this treatment value. Treatment value requires an eligible CAD phenotype and therapy actually started and tolerated.
If selected with another anti-inflammatory therapy, assign shared residual-inflammation C1 value once and count combined medication harm and monitoring burden explicitly.

citation.source_text: Tardif JC, Kouz S, Waters DD, et al. Efficacy and Safety of Low-Dose Colchicine after Myocardial Infarction. N Engl J Med. 2019;381(26):2497-2505. LoDoCo2 chronic coronary disease evidence: Nidorf SM, Fiolet ATL, Mosterd A, et al. N Engl J Med. 2020;383:1838-1847. PMID 32865380.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert HR 0.77 into relative risk reduction 0.23 for a narrow MACE endpoint; apply only after CAD residual-inflammatory phenotype eligibility is met.
evidence_trace.limitations: Trial-level effect does not automatically generalize to all elevated hs-CRP patients; interaction, renal/hepatic, GI, myopathy, and infection considerations remain patient-specific gates.
