citation.source_text: COLCOT enrolled recent-MI patients and reported MACE over a median follow-up near 2 years; LoDoCo2 supports chronic coronary disease directionality. The valuation binds only to that CAD/post-MI residual-risk phenotype.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use a 2.5-year matched-phenotype MACE baseline-risk band of 0.04 to 0.14, central 0.08, before applying the relative effect.
evidence_trace.limitations: This is a trial-phenotype baseline-risk binding, not a live patient-specific MACE model. Replace with individualized CAD risk when validated.

evidence_trace.transformation: Composite MACE event value uses the D2 event-value method for CV death, nonfatal MI, ischemic stroke, and urgent revascularization with low/central/high bands and no legacy qaly_per_event scalar.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal event-value model, not directly trial-measured utility; confidence remains model-translated and medium-capped.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by the event_or_curve_shift_action runner from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and the explicit horizon.
evidence_trace.limitations: Only narrow MACE prevention is credited; C2 and C3 are honest zeroes.

evidence_trace.transformation: Daily oral medication burden is integrated over the 2.5-year horizon after event-value computation, with monitoring, intolerance, interaction, and access burden represented as per-year QALY loss.
evidence_trace.limitations: Burden magnitude is internally calibrated and should be upgraded with patient-specific adverse-effect risk and preference data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Randomized CAD/post-MI evidence supports a narrow MACE effect, but patient QALY value is runner-derived from explicit phenotype baseline risk, internal event-value translation, and burden cadence, so confidence remains medium-capped.

applies_when.interpretation: Applies only when pregnancy is present and gestational diabetes is screen-detected or otherwise confirmed, with treatment to pregnancy glycemic targets clinically appropriate; baseline risk is bound to a treated-eligible GDM pregnancy phenotype rather than all pregnancies.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes baseline serious-perinatal-complication risk * relative_risk_reduction * dyad event_value_qaly. C2 anxiety/capacity and C3 neonatal resilience are honest zeroes until separately sourced.

c2_capacity.basis: No direct pregnancy capacity or anxiety-relief utility is credited in this promoted record.

c3_resilience.basis: No separate neonatal or maternal resilience channel is credited; the dyad value is confined to prevented serious perinatal complications.

burden_qaly.basis: One pregnancy episode: diagnostic testing, nutrition visits, home glucose checks or CGM, obstetric surveillance, medication escalation when needed, delivery planning, and postpartum testing.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: serious perinatal complications including perinatal death, shoulder dystocia, bone fracture, and nerve palsy via screen-detect-and-treat of gestational diabetes

basis.baseline_event_risk: Per-pregnancy serious-complication risk band for screen-detected or confirmed GDM eligible for active treatment; central value is phenotype-bound to the ACHOIS-style treated population rather than the general obstetric population.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: ACHOIS treatment effect converted from RR 0.33 to RRR 0.67, widened for diagnostic threshold, gestational age, and contemporary obstetric management.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal dyad event-value object for the serious perinatal composite; central value preserves the draft dyad anchor while low/high represent nonfatal injury through perinatal death or durable neonatal morbidity.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Pregnancy episode horizon through delivery; no lifetime postpartum diabetes or child metabolic value is credited.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - one_time burden_qaly.central

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands include baseline obstetric risk, diagnostic threshold, gestational age at detection, treatment achievement, dyad event-value range, and episode burden. Future maternal type 2 diabetes prevention is not included.
overlap_rules: Do not double count serious perinatal-complication prevention with general prenatal care, nutrition, obesity treatment, or other GDM treatment records.
Future maternal type 2 diabetes prevention must be modeled separately from this per-pregnancy dyad endpoint.
Screening VOI and direct treatment value remain separate; this record only credits screen-detected or confirmed GDM that leads to effective treatment.

citation.source_text: Crowther CA, Hiller JE, Moss JR, et al. Effect of treatment of gestational diabetes mellitus on pregnancy outcomes. N Engl J Med. 2005;352(24):2477-2486.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert RR 0.33 into central relative risk reduction 0.67 and widen to 0.45-0.80 for diagnostic and implementation heterogeneity.
evidence_trace.limitations: Effect depends on diagnostic criteria, gestational timing, treatment intensity, adherence, and local obstetric management.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind the action to a screen-detected/confirmed GDM pregnancy phenotype and use a per-pregnancy serious-complication risk band for runner computation.
evidence_trace.limitations: This is a phenotype-level baseline risk, not a live obstetric risk model; replace with patient-specific maternal-fetal risk when available.

evidence_trace.transformation: Translate a serious perinatal composite into a dyad QALY event-value band and exclude postpartum diabetes or child lifetime metabolic value.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal dyad value model pending direct maternal, neonatal, and family utility sources; medium-capped as model translated.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, and event_value_qaly.
evidence_trace.limitations: No C2 or C3 benefit is credited and no long-horizon postpartum value is included.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply one-time pregnancy episode burden after the dyad event-value computation.
evidence_trace.limitations: Burden calibration remains internal and should be upgraded with patient time, glucose-monitoring, medication, and visit-frequency data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Randomized trial evidence supports serious perinatal-complication reduction, but absolute patient value is phenotype-bound and model-translated through a dyad event-value object and internal burden calibration.

applies_when.interpretation: Applies only for adults with obesity and elevated diabetes risk, especially impaired glucose tolerance or prediabetes, where orlistat plus lifestyle support is clinically appropriate and competing anti-obesity therapy is not already claiming the same diabetes-prevention value.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes 4-year incident-T2DM baseline risk * relative_risk_reduction * event_value_qaly. Weight-related C2 is not credited to avoid overlap with obesity bundles.

c2_capacity.basis: No mobility, weight-symptom, or vitality utility is credited; those effects belong in obesity treatment records or bundles.

c3_resilience.basis: No separate metabolic resilience channel is credited.

burden_qaly.basis: Daily medication with meals, dietary fat restriction, GI adverse effects, vitamin supplementation, adherence failure, cost, and metabolic follow-up.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: incident type 2 diabetes in adults with obesity, with effect concentrated in impaired glucose tolerance or high baseline-risk subgroups

basis.baseline_event_risk: Four-year incident diabetes risk band for obesity with elevated glycemic risk; central value mirrors XENDOS-style high-risk obesity rather than average-risk obesity.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: XENDOS HR 0.63 converted to RRR 0.37 and widened for impaired-glucose-tolerance enrichment, adherence, and modern comparator uncertainty.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal incident-T2DM event-value band, restricted to onset delay and early diabetes burden without adding downstream ASCVD, CKD, weight, or sleep-apnea benefits.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Matches the randomized evidence horizon; per-year burden is integrated over 4 years.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly.central * sustained_horizon_years

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands include baseline diabetes risk, impaired-glucose-tolerance enrichment, adherence and persistence, GI intolerance, event-value range, and competing obesity-treatment overlap. Central net value may be negative after burden.
overlap_rules: Do not double count incident diabetes prevention with intensive lifestyle, GLP-1/GIP therapy, bariatric surgery, Mediterranean diet, or insulin-resistance lifestyle records.
Weight-related C2 capacity value is zero here and must be credited only once across obesity interventions.
ASCVD, CKD, MASLD, sleep-apnea, and cancer effects from weight loss require separate endpoint partitioning.

citation.source_text: Torgerson JS, Hauptman J, Boldrin MN, Sjostrom L. XENDOS: orlistat as an adjunct to lifestyle changes for prevention of type 2 diabetes in obese patients. Diabetes Care. 2004;27(1):155-161.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert HR 0.63 into central relative risk reduction 0.37 and widen to 0.20-0.55 for IGT enrichment and adherence uncertainty.
evidence_trace.limitations: Benefit is concentrated in higher-risk glycemic subgroups and may be dominated by newer obesity-treatment alternatives.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind baseline risk to a 4-year obesity plus elevated-glycemic-risk phenotype before applying the XENDOS relative effect.
evidence_trace.limitations: Phenotype-level baseline risk should be replaced by patient-specific diabetes risk from a validated metabolic risk model.

evidence_trace.transformation: Translate avoided incident type 2 diabetes into a bounded patient QALY event value without crediting later ASCVD, CKD, or weight-related capacity effects.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal event-value model pending direct utility and complication-partition upgrades; medium-capped as model translated.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and the explicit horizon.
evidence_trace.limitations: No C2/C3 value is credited and central net value can be negative after per-year burden integration.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply per-year medication and GI burden over the 4-year XENDOS horizon after event-value computation.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration pending direct GI adverse-effect, discontinuation, preference, and cost data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Randomized diabetes-incidence evidence exists, but value is small, burden-sensitive, subgroup-dependent, and model-translated from a phenotype baseline risk and internal event-value object.

applies_when.interpretation: Applies only for postmenopausal women with elevated validated breast-cancer risk, a meaningful ER-positive preventable fraction, acceptable bone/fracture risk, and willingness to consider aromatase-inhibitor chemoprevention.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes 5-year invasive-breast-cancer baseline risk * relative_risk_reduction * event_value_qaly. Chemoprevention symptoms and bone harms are handled as burden, not positive C2.

c2_capacity.basis: No positive capacity channel is credited for aromatase-inhibitor chemoprevention.

c3_resilience.basis: No downstream therapy-intensity or recovery resilience value is credited.

burden_qaly.basis: Daily aromatase inhibitor, arthralgia, vasomotor and sexual symptoms, bone-density monitoring, fracture-risk management, adherence/discontinuation burden, and shared-decision follow-up.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: invasive breast cancer incidence in high-risk postmenopausal women treated with aromatase-inhibitor chemoprevention

basis.baseline_event_risk: Five-year invasive breast cancer risk band for a validated high-risk, postmenopausal, non-BRCA chemoprevention phenotype; central value requires elevated absolute risk rather than average population risk.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: IBIS-II anastrozole HR 0.51 converted to central RRR 0.49 and widened for adherence, discontinuation, and ER-positive preventable-fraction uncertainty.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal invasive-breast-cancer event-value band covering diagnosis, treatment morbidity, recurrence/mortality risk, and quality loss; no screening-detection value is included.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Per-year burden is integrated over a 5-year prevention course; longer carryover benefit is not extrapolated.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly.central * sustained_horizon_years

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands include absolute breast-cancer risk, ER-positive preventable fraction, adherence, discontinuation, baseline bone/fracture risk, symptom burden, event-value range, and overlap with screening or genetic-risk interventions. Central net value may be negative after burden.
overlap_rules: Do not double count cancer-incidence or mortality value with breast MRI screening, tamoxifen, risk-reducing surgery, BRCA pathways, or MCED workup.
Bone-loss harms and fracture prevention must overlap with osteoporosis treatment records when both are active.
Screening detection value and chemoprevention incidence reduction must remain separate.

citation.source_text: Cuzick J, Sestak I, Forbes JF, et al. Use of anastrozole for breast cancer prevention (IBIS-II): long-term results of a randomised controlled trial. Lancet. 2020;395(10218):117-122.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert HR 0.51 into central relative risk reduction 0.49 and widen to 0.35-0.60 for adherence, discontinuation, and preventable-fraction uncertainty.
evidence_trace.limitations: Benefit depends on high absolute risk, postmenopausal status, ER-positive fraction, adherence, symptom tolerability, and bone safety.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind baseline risk to a validated high-risk postmenopausal chemoprevention phenotype before applying the IBIS-II relative effect.
evidence_trace.limitations: Phenotype-level baseline risk should be replaced by a live validated 5-year and lifetime breast cancer risk model.

evidence_trace.transformation: Translate prevented invasive breast cancer into an event-value band and exclude screening reclassification value.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal cancer event-value model pending direct utility, stage, treatment-intensity, recurrence, and mortality partition upgrades.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and the explicit horizon.
evidence_trace.limitations: No positive capacity or resilience value is credited; central net value can be negative after symptom and bone burden.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply per-year aromatase-inhibitor symptom, bone, monitoring, and shared-decision burden over a 5-year prevention course.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration pending direct symptom, discontinuation, fracture, preference, and adherence data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Randomized prevention evidence supports invasive breast-cancer incidence reduction in high-risk postmenopausal women, but absolute patient value is baseline-risk, adherence, bone, symptom, and event-value-model dependent.

applies_when.interpretation: Applies only for women with elevated validated breast-cancer risk who are clinically eligible for SERM chemoprevention after menopausal status, VTE/stroke risk, uterus/endometrial context, vasomotor symptoms, and patient preference are reviewed. Do not use for average-risk patients or when aromatase-inhibitor chemoprevention, BRCA surgery/surveillance, or screening VOI supersedes the decision.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes 5-year invasive-breast-cancer baseline risk * SERM relative risk reduction * event_value_qaly. VTE, endometrial, vasomotor, and medication burdens are handled as burden rather than positive C2.

c2_capacity.basis: No positive capacity channel is credited for preventive SERM therapy; vasomotor, sexual, and thromboembolic/endometrial concerns belong in burden and eligibility.

c3_resilience.basis: No separate resilience credit; downstream treatment-intensity reduction is not independently parameterized.

burden_qaly.basis: Daily SERM therapy, hot flashes/vasomotor symptoms, VTE and stroke concern, endometrial evaluation when uterus present, cataract/sexual-symptom concerns, adherence/discontinuation burden, and shared-decision follow-up.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: invasive breast cancer incidence in elevated-risk women treated with SERM chemoprevention

basis.baseline_event_risk: Five-year invasive breast cancer risk band for a validated elevated-risk non-BRCA chemoprevention phenotype; central value requires risk above usual screening-only thresholds, not average population risk.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Cuzick 2013 individual-participant SERM prevention meta-analysis, HR about 0.62 for breast cancer, converted to central RRR 0.38 and widened for drug choice, menopausal status, adherence, discontinuation, and ER-positive preventable-fraction uncertainty.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal invasive-breast-cancer event-value band covering diagnosis, treatment morbidity, recurrence/mortality risk, and quality loss; screening reclassification value is excluded.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Per-year burden is integrated over a 5-year prevention course; carryover benefit beyond the bounded decision horizon is not extrapolated.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly.central * sustained_horizon_years

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Randomized prevention evidence supports invasive breast-cancer incidence reduction in elevated-risk women, but absolute patient value is baseline-risk, drug choice, menopausal/uterus/VTE context, adherence, event-value-model, and burden dependent.
overlap_rules: Do not double count with aromatase-inhibitor chemoprevention, BRCA risk-reducing surgery, BRCA MRI surveillance, or breast screening VOI pathways unless a residual-risk model encodes sequencing.
Use only one breast-cancer chemoprevention row as the active recommendation unless explicit incremental or switch logic is modeled.
Do not use for VUS-only genetic findings or average-risk screening contexts.

citation.source_text: Cuzick J, Sestak I, Bonanni B, et al. Selective oestrogen receptor modulators in prevention of breast cancer: an updated meta-analysis of individual participant data. Lancet. 2013;381(9880):1827-1834.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert SERM meta-analysis HR about 0.62 into central relative risk reduction 0.38 and widen to 0.25-0.50 for SERM choice, adherence, discontinuation, and preventable-fraction uncertainty.
evidence_trace.limitations: Benefit depends on elevated absolute risk, ER-positive preventable fraction, menopausal and uterus context, and SERM-specific harm profile.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind baseline risk to a validated elevated-risk non-BRCA breast-cancer chemoprevention phenotype before applying SERM relative effect.
evidence_trace.limitations: Phenotype-level baseline risk should be replaced by a live validated 5-year and lifetime breast cancer risk model.

evidence_trace.transformation: Translate prevented invasive breast cancer into an event-value band and exclude screening reclassification value.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal cancer event-value model pending direct utility, stage, treatment-intensity, recurrence, and mortality partition upgrades.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and the explicit horizon.
evidence_trace.limitations: No positive capacity or resilience value is credited; central net value can be negative after SERM symptom and harm burden.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply per-year SERM symptom, thromboembolic/endometrial-risk, monitoring, and shared-decision burden over a 5-year prevention course.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration pending direct symptom, discontinuation, VTE/endometrial event, preference, and adherence data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Randomized/meta-analytic prevention evidence supports invasive breast-cancer incidence reduction in elevated-risk women, but absolute QALY is medium-capped by baseline-risk binding, event-value translation, SERM-specific harms, and burden calibration.

applies_when.interpretation: Applies only when CKD progression risk is materially BP-driven, baseline BP remains above the individualized intensive target, and orthostasis, frailty, AKI, potassium, and medication-stack risks make intensification clinically reasonable.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes 3-year CKD/CVD composite baseline risk * intensive-BP relative risk reduction * cardiorenal event_value_qaly. No separate capacity or resilience value is credited.

c2_capacity.basis: No direct felt-capacity benefit is credited; dizziness, fatigue, falls, and polypharmacy are handled as burden or harm.

c3_resilience.basis: No separate residual-event resilience value is credited without an independent severity or recovery anchor.

burden_qaly.basis: Daily antihypertensive intensification, home BP monitoring, post-titration creatinine/electrolyte checks, orthostasis review, fall/AKI surveillance, and periodic target reassessment. Burden is integrated over the 3-year evidence horizon.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: CKD progression, kidney failure, cardiovascular events, or death among CKD patients where BP is a material modifiable driver

basis.baseline_event_risk: Phenotype-bound 3-year composite CKD/CVD event-risk band for hypertensive CKD with meaningful residual BP-driven risk; it is not an average population risk and must be replaced by live CKD/CVD baseline-risk binding when available.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Lv et al. intensive BP lowering meta-analysis HR 0.79 converted to central RRR 0.21 and widened for achieved BP separation, target selection, and harm guardrails.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal D2 cardiorenal composite event-value band covering sustained eGFR decline, kidney failure, MI/stroke/CV death mixture, and hospitalization morbidity without adding a separate resilience channel.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Per-year burden is integrated over 3 years to match a near-term BP-intensification evidence and reassessment horizon rather than extrapolated lifetime therapy.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly.central * sustained_horizon_years

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands include baseline CKD/CVD risk, achieved BP separation, albuminuria and eGFR phenotype, endpoint mix, event-value model, hypotension/fall/AKI/electrolyte harms, medication burden, and overlap with other kidney-protective therapies.
overlap_rules: Do not double count CKD or CV event reduction with RAAS blockade, SGLT2 inhibitors, finerenone, sodium reduction, or other BP-lowering records unless an explicit incremental residual-risk slice is modeled.
If paired with lifestyle BP lowering or dietary sodium/protein CKD actions, partition achieved BP reduction by intervention and count home-monitoring burden once.
Hypotension, fall, AKI, and electrolyte harms must be modeled once across BP-intensification actions.

citation.source_text: Lv J, Ehteshami P, Sarnak MJ, et al. Effects of intensive blood pressure lowering on the progression of chronic kidney disease: a systematic review and meta-analysis. CMAJ. 2013;185(11):949-957.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert HR 0.79 into central relative risk reduction 0.21 and widen to 0.10-0.30 for achieved BP separation, target selection, and patient tolerance uncertainty.
evidence_trace.limitations: Absolute value depends on hypertensive CKD phenotype, baseline eGFR/albuminuria, achieved BP, competing risks, background therapy, and adverse effects.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind to a hypertensive CKD phenotype with meaningful residual BP-driven 3-year CKD/CVD event risk before applying the relative effect.
evidence_trace.limitations: Phenotype-level baseline risk is an explicit audited placeholder pending live patient-specific CKD progression and CVD risk binding.

evidence_trace.transformation: Translate prevented cardiorenal composite events into a QALY event-value band spanning sustained eGFR decline, kidney failure, MI/stroke/CV death, and hospitalization morbidity.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal event-value model pending endpoint-specific kidney, CV, hospitalization, and AKI event-value partitioning.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and explicit horizon-bound burden.
evidence_trace.limitations: No C2 or C3 value is credited and the generic runner does not yet decompose kidney and cardiovascular endpoints separately.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply annual antihypertensive-intensification, home-monitoring, lab, orthostasis, and adverse-effect burden over the 3-year reassessment horizon.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration pending regimen-specific pill count, monitoring frequency, adverse-event, frailty, and preference data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Meta-analytic CKD BP-lowering evidence supports directionality, but absolute patient value is model-translated through phenotype-level baseline risk, composite event value, harms, burden, and overlap handling.

applies_when.interpretation: Applies when reduced eGFR, AKI risk, or a transition of care creates a real opportunity to review active renally cleared or nephrotoxic medications and make an accepted medication change. Do not score when medication data are unavailable or no actionable exposure exists.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes episode-level AKI/toxicity/hospitalization baseline risk * accepted-review relative risk reduction * event_value_qaly. Diagnostic/safety VOI is represented by the actionable-exposure gate rather than a separate unrunnered value.

c2_capacity.basis: No direct capacity benefit is credited without a sourced symptom-to-utility anchor for avoided drug toxicity.

c3_resilience.basis: No separate resilience credit is included; fewer iatrogenic complications are counted through C1 event prevention.

burden_qaly.basis: Medication reconciliation, clinician or pharmacist review, patient communication, follow-up labs when changes are made, substitution risk, and alert-fatigue/false-positive burden for one review episode.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: AKI progression, severe medication toxicity, hospitalization, dialysis transition, or death preventable by an accepted eGFR-aware medication review

actionable_safety_voi_inputs.gate: active renal-dose-sensitive or nephrotoxic medication exposure plus clinician acceptance of a management change
actionable_safety_voi_inputs.value_rule: No VOI or event-prevention value is credited if medication-list review finds no actionable exposure or if the result does not change management.

basis.baseline_event_risk: Episode-level AKI/toxicity/hospitalization risk for a low-eGFR or AKI-risk medication-review phenotype with an actionable renal-dose or nephrotoxin exposure.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Wilson et al. automated medication-targeted alert trial RR 0.92 converted to central RRR 0.08 and widened for alert acceptance, actionable-yield, and outpatient generalizability uncertainty.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal D2 medication-safety event-value band spanning transient AKI/toxicity, hospitalization, temporary dialysis, chronic dialysis transition, and death mixture.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: One medication-review episode; burden is one-time and not lifetime integrated.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - one_time burden_qaly.central

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands include actionable exposure prevalence, medication-list completeness, alert/review acceptance, baseline AKI/toxicity risk, substituted-therapy harms, false-positive burden, event-value model, and inpatient-to-outpatient generalizability.
overlap_rules: Do not double count avoided AKI or medication toxicity with nephrology referral, CKD care management, pharmacy review, contrast safety, inpatient CDS, or transitions-of-care medication reconciliation.
If medication review changes RAAS/SGLT2/diuretic therapy, count disease-modifying gain or loss under the affected therapy record, not again here.
Count chart review, patient communication, follow-up labs, and alert-fatigue burden once across medication-safety actions in the same episode.

citation.source_text: Wilson FP, Yamamoto Y, Martin M, et al. A randomized clinical trial assessing the effect of automated medication-targeted alerts on acute kidney injury outcomes (ELAIA-2). Nat Commun. 2023;14(1):2826.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert RR 0.92 into central relative risk reduction 0.08 and widen to 0.02-0.14 for actionable exposure, alert acceptance, and setting generalizability.
evidence_trace.limitations: Alert-trial mechanism and small effect are conditional on medication exposure, clinician response, and local implementation; no value is credited without an actionable medication gate.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind to a low-eGFR or AKI-risk medication-review episode with an actionable renal-dose or nephrotoxin exposure before applying the alert/review effect.
evidence_trace.limitations: Phenotype-level baseline risk and actionable-yield gate should be replaced by live medication-list, eGFR trajectory, and setting-specific risk models.

evidence_trace.transformation: Translate prevented AKI/toxicity/hospitalization/dialysis/death mixture into an internal medication-safety event-value band.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal event-value model pending endpoint-specific AKI, toxicity, hospitalization, dialysis, and mortality utility partitioning.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from the actionable-exposure baseline risk, relative risk reduction, event_value_qaly, and one-time review burden.
evidence_trace.limitations: The generic runner captures direct event-prevention value; it does not yet emit a separate displayed VOI object for actionable medication discovery.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply one-time burden for medication reconciliation, clinician/pharmacist review, communication, follow-up labs, substitution risk, and false-positive/alert fatigue.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration pending direct setting-specific review-time, alert-fatigue, medication-count, and patient-communication data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Randomized alert evidence supports a safety mechanism, but patient value is small and highly conditional on an actionable medication exposure, accepted management change, baseline risk, event-value translation, and review burden.

applies_when.interpretation: Narrow map-eligible valuation for a recent-onset, non-insulin type 2 diabetes phenotype where structured intentional weight loss can plausibly induce remission. Requires diabetes duration, medication status, BMI, HbA1c, contraindications, and eating-disorder/frailty safety checks before recommendation.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes remission/downstream-complication C1 from a recent-onset T2DM baseline composite risk, remission-associated relative risk reduction, and diabetes-state/complication event_value_qaly. Medication reduction, mobility, and vitality C2 are honest zeroes pending sourced utilities.

c2_capacity.basis: Honest zero: no sourced, separately de-duplicated C2 capacity utility anchor is credited in this narrow promotion.

c3_resilience.basis: Honest zero: no sourced residual-event severity, recovery, or resilience input is credited separately from C1.

burden_qaly.basis: Per-year burden for DiRECT-like structured weight-loss induction, meal replacement or strict dietary protocol, glucose medication adjustment, monitoring, relapse prevention, hunger/social friction, gallstone and hypoglycemia risk. Integrated over the 2-year evidence horizon.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: recent-onset type 2 diabetes remission and downstream diabetes-state or micro/macrovascular complication burden avoided through structured intentional weight loss

basis.baseline_event_risk: Recent-onset T2DM remission-responsive phenotype over 2 years, binding the record to non-insulin/recoverable beta-cell context rather than all diabetes.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Legacy RR 0.66 converted to central RRR 0.34 for remaining diabetic state/downstream risk; bands widened for diabetes duration, achieved weight loss, relapse, and medication context.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal D2 diabetes-state and complication composite value, narrower than full lifetime diabetes burden and excluding separate C2 capacity utility.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Matches DiRECT 2-year remission durability evidence and per-year program burden.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly at declared cadence

cohort_projection.display_note: Narrow Batch 018 promotion; engine computes C1 from derivation_inputs and subtracts burden at the declared cadence and horizon.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands include diabetes duration, beta-cell reserve, achieved and maintained weight loss, relapse, medication changes, hypoglycemia/gallstone/eating-disorder safety, event-value uncertainty, burden, and overlap with other weight-loss or glycemic-control actions.
overlap_rules: Do not double count with GLP-1/GIP or bariatric weight loss, metformin/glycemic-control records, DPP lifestyle, Mediterranean diet, physical activity, or medication de-intensification unless remission, complication, and burden slices are partitioned.
Shared coaching, diet, monitoring, weight-loss burden, and medication-adjustment burden must be counted once across selected metabolic lifestyle actions.

citation.source_text: Lean MEJ, Leslie WS, Barnes AC, et al. Primary care-led weight management for remission of type 2 diabetes (DiRECT). Lancet. 2018;391(10120):541-551.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert legacy RR 0.66 to central RRR 0.34 for the remaining diabetic state/downstream-risk composite; widen to 0.10-0.55 for remission durability, diabetes duration, and achieved weight loss.
evidence_trace.limitations: Open-label cluster trial; QALY value is translated through remission/downstream-risk model rather than directly measured lifetime QALY.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind to a recent-onset T2DM phenotype with remission-responsive beta-cell reserve over 2 years, not all undiagnosed or long-standing diabetes.
evidence_trace.limitations: Explicit phenotype band, not a live patient-specific diabetes-complication risk model.

evidence_trace.transformation: Translate remission and avoided diabetes-state/complication burden into a conservative D2 event_value_qaly band while excluding separate medication-reduction, mobility, or vitality C2 value.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal D2 event-value model, not directly trial-measured utility; confidence remains capped for model-translated value.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and explicit burden cadence.
evidence_trace.limitations: Generic event runner only; C2/C3 lifestyle capacity, adherence, cost, and overlap effects are not added unless separately sourced.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply per-year high-intensity weight-loss/remission-program burden over the 2-year evidence horizon.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration pending patient-time, acceptability, adverse-event, and access data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: DiRECT supports remission through structured weight loss, but patient QALY is runner-derived from phenotype-bound baseline risk, internal event-value translation, remission durability, burden, and overlap controls.

applies_when.interpretation: Narrow map-eligible valuation for a sedentary or low-activity prediabetes phenotype where a sustained leisure-time activity increase is feasible. Requires baseline activity, achieved MET-hours per week, mobility/cardiac safety, injury risk, and overlap with any broader physical-activity plan before recommendation.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes incident-diabetes C1 from prediabetes baseline transition risk, achieved-activity relative risk reduction, and incident-diabetes event_value_qaly. General energy, mood, sleep, fitness, and function C2 are honest zeroes here because the physical_activity bundle already carries shared capacity value.

c2_capacity.basis: Honest zero: no sourced, separately de-duplicated C2 capacity utility anchor is credited in this narrow promotion.

c3_resilience.basis: Honest zero: no sourced residual-event severity, recovery, or resilience input is credited separately from C1.

burden_qaly.basis: Per-year burden for activity prescription, time, soreness, injury risk, equipment/access, tracking, safety screening, coaching, and relapse. Integrated over a 3-year prevention horizon.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: incident type 2 diabetes prevented in a prediabetes/low-activity phenotype through sustained leisure-time physical activity increase

basis.baseline_event_risk: DPP-like prediabetes transition-risk band over 3 years, explicitly requiring prediabetes and feasible activity change rather than applying to all adults.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Smith 2016 dose-response meta-analysis central RR about 0.74 at moderate activity dose converted to RRR 0.26; bands widened for observational confounding, baseline sedentariness, achieved dose, and durability.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal D2 incident-diabetes event value for diagnosis and early morbidity avoided, excluding separate fitness C2 value.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Three-year diabetes-prevention horizon; burden is per-year over the same horizon.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly at declared cadence

cohort_projection.display_note: Narrow Batch 018 promotion; engine computes C1 from derivation_inputs and subtracts burden at the declared cadence and horizon.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands include baseline sedentariness, achieved MET-hours, adherence durability, residual confounding, prediabetes transition risk, incident-diabetes event value, injury/safety burden, and overlap with physical-activity, weight-loss, and diet bundles.
overlap_rules: Do not double count incident-diabetes prevention with DPP lifestyle, metformin prevention, GLP-1/GIP weight loss, direct weight remission, Mediterranean diet, or other weight-loss records unless endpoint slices are partitioned.

Do not add general activity C2 capacity here when the physical_activity bundle or resistance/aerobic prescriptions already credit shared fitness capacity. Count activity time, coaching, soreness, and injury burden once.

citation.source_text: Smith AD, Crippa A, Woodcock J, Brage S. Physical activity and incident type 2 diabetes mellitus: a systematic review and dose-response meta-analysis. Diabetologia. 2016;59(12):2527-2545.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert RR about 0.74 at moderate leisure-time activity dose into central RRR 0.26; widen to 0.10-0.45 for baseline activity, achieved MET-hours, confounding, and durability.
evidence_trace.limitations: Mostly prospective cohorts; absolute patient value requires prediabetes baseline risk and achieved-dose binding.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind to a 3-year prediabetes transition-risk band before applying activity RRR.
evidence_trace.limitations: Explicit phenotype band, not yet a live individualized diabetes-risk model with baseline MET-hours.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use a conservative D2 incident-diabetes event_value_qaly band for diagnosis and early morbidity avoided; shared activity capacity value is excluded to prevent double counting.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal D2 event-value model, not directly trial-measured utility; confidence remains capped for model-translated value.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and explicit burden cadence.
evidence_trace.limitations: Generic event runner only; C2/C3 lifestyle capacity, adherence, cost, and overlap effects are not added unless separately sourced.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply per-year activity burden over a 3-year diabetes-prevention horizon, including time, soreness, injury risk, access, tracking, and coaching.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration pending patient-time, acceptability, adverse-event, and access data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Dose-response evidence supports activity for diabetes prevention, but this narrow valuation is medium-capped because absolute QALY is phenotype-bound, model-translated, burden-calibrated internally, and excludes shared activity C2 to avoid overlap.

applies_when.interpretation: Narrow low-confidence valuation for low-fiber or low-whole-grain intake where a sustained +8 g/day fiber-equivalent change is realistic and GI tolerance is acceptable. Requires baseline intake, food access, diet-quality context, and whether the change is part of a broader diet bundle before recommendation.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes a conservative composite C1 for CVD, incident diabetes, colorectal cancer, or serious diet-sensitive morbidity from baseline endpoint-risk basket, fiber/whole-grain relative risk reduction, and composite event_value_qaly. Satiety, bowel-function, glycemic variability, and energy C2 are honest zeroes pending sourced utilities and GI intolerance netting.

c2_capacity.basis: Honest zero: no sourced, separately de-duplicated C2 capacity utility anchor is credited in this narrow promotion.

c3_resilience.basis: Honest zero: no sourced residual-event severity, recovery, or resilience input is credited separately from C1.

burden_qaly.basis: Per-year burden for daily food substitution, grocery/cooking changes, taste and household friction, gradual fiber titration, tracking, cost/access, bloating or GI intolerance, and maintenance. Integrated over a 5-year diet-exposure horizon.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: composite CVD, incident diabetes, colorectal cancer, or serious diet-sensitive morbidity prevented through sustained dietary fiber or whole-grain intake increase

basis.baseline_event_risk: Five-year composite endpoint-risk basket for a low-fiber/suboptimal-diet phenotype; this is an explicit basket binding, not a patient-specific multiendpoint model.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Reynolds 2019 carbohydrate-quality meta-analysis supports direction and dose-response; central RRR 0.10 is narrowed and widened to 0.03-0.18 for observational confounding, endpoint mix, baseline intake, and achieved +8 g/day dose.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal D2 composite event value for CVD, incident diabetes, colorectal cancer, and serious morbidity; does not include C2 bowel/satiety/energy utility.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Five-year exposure horizon for diet-substitution benefit and per-year burden.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly at declared cadence

cohort_projection.display_note: Narrow Batch 018 promotion; engine computes C1 from derivation_inputs and subtracts burden at the declared cadence and horizon.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands include baseline fiber and whole-grain intake, achieved dose, endpoint mix, diet-quality confounding, five-year baseline risks, internal composite event value, GI intolerance, food access, and overlap with other diet-quality interventions.
overlap_rules: Do not double count with Mediterranean diet, DASH/sodium changes, processed/red meat reduction, weight-loss diets, diabetes-prevention lifestyle, or colorectal-cancer nutrition records unless endpoint and achieved-intake slices are partitioned.
Count grocery, cooking, tracking, household-friction, GI intolerance, and diet-coaching burden once across a selected nutrition plan.

citation.source_text: Reynolds A, Mann J, Cummings J, Winter N, Mete E, Te Morenga L. Carbohydrate quality and human health: a series of systematic reviews and meta-analyses. Lancet. 2019;393(10170):434-445.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use a conservative central RRR 0.10 for a fiber/whole-grain composite endpoint basket, with bands 0.03-0.18 for endpoint mix, achieved dose, baseline intake, and confounding.
evidence_trace.limitations: Evidence mixes randomized risk factors and observational endpoints; patient value is low-confidence and requires baseline-intake and diet-overlap controls.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind to a low-fiber/suboptimal-diet phenotype with a five-year composite endpoint risk basket before applying the conservative fiber RRR.
evidence_trace.limitations: Basket baseline risk is internal and should be replaced with endpoint-specific patient risk models.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use a D2 composite event_value_qaly band for CVD, incident diabetes, colorectal cancer, and serious diet-sensitive morbidity while excluding separate bowel, satiety, energy, or glycemic-variability C2 utility.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal D2 event-value model, not directly trial-measured utility; confidence remains capped for model-translated value.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and explicit burden cadence.
evidence_trace.limitations: Generic event runner only; C2/C3 lifestyle capacity, adherence, cost, and overlap effects are not added unless separately sourced.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply per-year nutrition substitution and GI-titration burden over a 5-year diet-exposure horizon.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration pending patient-time, acceptability, adverse-event, and access data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Fiber and whole-grain evidence is directionally supportive and dose-responsive, but patient QALY is low-confidence because the endpoint basket, baseline risk, event value, diet confounding, GI burden, and nutrition overlap are internally translated.

applies_when.interpretation: Applies narrowly to a confirmed pathogenic or likely pathogenic BRCA1/BRCA2 carrier with breast tissue present who is considering bilateral risk-reducing mastectomy after genetics and surgical counseling. Do not use for VUS-only findings, unconfirmed family history, prior bilateral mastectomy, active breast cancer treatment, or patients who would not consider surgery.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes BRCA-carrier breast-cancer baseline risk * RRR from PROSE * internal breast-cancer event-value band. No ovarian-risk benefit is bundled.

c2_capacity.basis: No generic capacity credit. Anxiety relief, body image, pain, sexual function, and surveillance relief are treated inside burden/preference uncertainty rather than a separate positive channel.

c3_resilience.basis: No separate resilience credit; treatment-intensity reduction after residual cancer is not independently parameterized.

burden_qaly.basis: One-time risk-reducing mastectomy and reconstruction decision burden, including operative recovery, complications, chronic pain/numbness, revision risk, body image, sexual function, surveillance change, and preference sensitivity. High band reflects patients for whom long-term sequelae dominate.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: invasive breast cancer incidence and downstream breast-cancer morbidity/mortality in confirmed BRCA1/BRCA2 pathogenic-variant carriers through bilateral risk-reducing mastectomy

basis.baseline_event_risk: Internal BRCA-carrier lifetime/long-horizon breast-cancer risk band for an eligible carrier with breast tissue present, intentionally broad for BRCA1 versus BRCA2, age, family history, prior cancer, and competing surveillance. This record is not scored when pathogenic status is unconfirmed.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: PROSE cohort effect carried forward as a risk-reducing mastectomy relative risk reduction band centered on 0.90.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal D2 breast-cancer event-value band spanning nonfatal treatment burden, recurrence/anxiety/treatment-quality loss, and mortality contribution; ovarian risk is excluded.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: One-time surgical intervention; horizon is present only to satisfy the shared event-action runner and does not annualize the surgical burden.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands combine BRCA1/BRCA2 and age-dependent absolute risk, PROSE relative-effect uncertainty, internal breast-cancer event-value translation, surgical/reconstruction burden, and patient-preference sensitivity. This is map-eligible only after confirmed P/LP BRCA status; upstream genetic testing remains a separate diagnostic VOI.
overlap_rules: Do not double count with BRCA breast MRI surveillance, mammography, chemoprevention, supplemental MRI, genetic testing, or downstream breast-cancer treatment.
Do not bundle ovarian cancer prevention or salpingo-oophorectomy value into this mastectomy record.
If prior breast cancer or contralateral-risk reduction is the active indication, use a separate contralateral/post-treatment model rather than this primary prophylaxis valuation.

citation.source_text: Rebbeck TR, Friebel T, Lynch HT, et al. Bilateral prophylactic mastectomy reduces breast cancer risk in BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutation carriers: the PROSE Study Group. J Clin Oncol. 2004;22(6):1055-1062.

evidence_trace.transformation: Carry the legacy PROSE risk-reduction estimate forward as a banded RRR 0.85/0.90/0.95 for confirmed pathogenic BRCA carriers; runner applies it only to the explicit absolute-risk band.
evidence_trace.limitations: Observational cohort, not randomized; net value depends on gene, age, breast tissue status, prior cancer, competing surveillance, and patient preferences.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use a broad confirmed-carrier breast-cancer risk band rather than applying the relative effect to an average-risk population. The gate requires confirmed BRCA P/LP status and breast tissue present.
evidence_trace.limitations: Not yet patient-specific by BRCA1 versus BRCA2, age, polygenic/family modifiers, prior cancer, MRI adherence, or competing mortality; confidence is medium-capped.

evidence_trace.transformation: Translate a prevented invasive breast-cancer event into patient QALY using the internal event-value method, including treatment morbidity and mortality contribution but excluding ovarian-risk benefits.
evidence_trace.limitations: Model-translated event value, not directly measured in PROSE; must be upgraded with stage-specific utility/survival modeling.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is runner-computed as baseline_event_risk * relative_risk_reduction * event_value_qaly with C2/C3 held at honest zero.
evidence_trace.limitations: Does not encode patient willingness, reconstruction choice, or surgical contraindications; those remain eligibility/preference axes.

evidence_trace.transformation: Model risk-reducing mastectomy as a one-time procedure burden with a wide band for reconstruction, complications, chronic pain/numbness, body image, sexual function, surveillance changes, and preference-sensitive tradeoffs.
evidence_trace.limitations: Burden remains internally calibrated and preference-sensitive; direct patient utility anchors are pending.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Large cohort-supported relative risk reduction in a genetically defined high-risk group, with medium confidence after absolute-risk, event-value, and surgical-burden translation.

applies_when.interpretation: Applies narrowly to a confirmed Lynch syndrome mismatch-repair pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant carrier with uterus and/or ovaries present who is considering risk-reducing hysterectomy with bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy or a staged gynecologic risk-reduction plan after genetics and gynecologic oncology counseling. Do not use for VUS-only findings, completed relevant surgery, active gynecologic cancer treatment, unresolved fertility plans, or patients who would not consider surgery.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes Lynch gynecologic cancer baseline risk * RRR from the prophylactic-surgery cohort * internal endometrial/ovarian cancer event-value band.

c2_capacity.basis: No generic capacity credit. Fertility, menopause, sexual function, anxiety relief, and surveillance relief are handled in burden/preference uncertainty.

c3_resilience.basis: No separate resilience credit; treatment-intensity changes after residual gynecologic cancer are not independently parameterized.

burden_qaly.basis: One-time gynecologic risk-reduction surgery burden, including operative recovery, complications, fertility loss, surgical menopause, hormone-therapy management, sexual function, pelvic floor effects, surveillance displacement, anxiety relief, and patient preference. High band reflects early/premenopausal or high-sequelae scenarios.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: endometrial and ovarian cancer incidence and downstream morbidity/mortality in confirmed Lynch syndrome carriers through risk-reducing hysterectomy with bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy or staged surgery

basis.baseline_event_risk: Internal Lynch carrier gynecologic cancer risk band for an eligible carrier with relevant organs present, intentionally broad for MSH2/MLH1/MSH6/PMS2/EPCAM, age, prior cancer, and surveillance context. This record is not scored when Lynch status is unconfirmed.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Schmeler et al. prophylactic-surgery cohort carried forward as a risk-reducing surgery relative risk reduction band centered on 0.90.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal D2 blended endometrial/ovarian cancer event-value band, with ovarian cancer severity represented in the high band and no colon-cancer benefit bundled.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: One-time surgical intervention; horizon is present only to satisfy the shared event-action runner and does not annualize surgical burden.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands combine MMR gene and age-dependent gynecologic cancer absolute risk, cohort relative-effect uncertainty, internal endometrial/ovarian cancer event-value translation, surgical menopause and fertility burden, hormone-therapy suitability, and preference sensitivity. Upstream Lynch testing remains a separate diagnostic VOI.
overlap_rules: Do not double count with Lynch colonoscopy surveillance, aspirin chemoprevention, endometrial/ovarian surveillance, BRCA ovarian risk-reduction, hereditary cancer router records, genetic testing, or downstream gynecologic cancer treatment.
Partition endometrial and ovarian risk-reduction from colon-cancer prevention and from external family cascade value.
If only hysterectomy or only oophorectomy is under consideration, use a staged procedure model rather than the combined central valuation.

citation.source_text: Schmeler KM, Lynch HT, Chen LM, et al. Prophylactic surgery to reduce the risk of gynecologic cancers in the Lynch syndrome. N Engl J Med. 2006;354(3):261-269.

evidence_trace.transformation: Carry the legacy prophylactic-surgery effect forward as a banded RRR 0.85/0.90/0.95 for confirmed Lynch carriers with relevant organs present; runner applies it only to the explicit gynecologic absolute-risk band.
evidence_trace.limitations: Observational cohort; net value depends on MMR gene, age, prior cancer, uterus/ovary status, fertility plans, menopause timing, hormone therapy, surveillance alternatives, and preferences.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use a broad confirmed-Lynch gynecologic cancer risk band rather than applying the relative effect to a general population. The gate requires confirmed pathogenic Lynch status and relevant organs present.
evidence_trace.limitations: Not yet patient-specific by MMR gene, current age, prior hysterectomy/oophorectomy, parity, family history, colon-cancer history, or competing mortality; confidence is medium-capped.

evidence_trace.transformation: Translate prevented endometrial/ovarian cancer into patient QALY using the internal event-value method; ovarian cancer severity is reflected in the high band while colon-cancer benefit is excluded.
evidence_trace.limitations: Model-translated event value, not directly measured in the cited cohort; should be upgraded with site-, stage-, age-, and treatment-specific utility/survival values.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is runner-computed as baseline_event_risk * relative_risk_reduction * event_value_qaly with C2/C3 held at honest zero.
evidence_trace.limitations: Does not encode surgical timing, fertility preference, hormone-therapy suitability, or contraindications; those remain eligibility/preference axes.

evidence_trace.transformation: Model risk-reducing hysterectomy/BSO or staged gynecologic risk reduction as a one-time procedure burden with a wide band for operative recovery, complications, fertility loss, menopause symptoms, hormone therapy, sexual function, chronic symptoms, anxiety relief, and preference-sensitive tradeoffs.
evidence_trace.limitations: Burden remains internally calibrated and highly timing/preference-sensitive; direct patient utility anchors are pending.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Cohort evidence supports a large gynecologic cancer risk-reduction effect in confirmed Lynch carriers, but absolute-risk, timing, event-value, and menopause/fertility burden translation keep confidence medium.

applies_when.interpretation: Narrow Batch 020 valuation for advancing, progressive, or high-risk CKD where early nephrology referral and multidisciplinary care are still actionable compared with late referral or unmanaged primary-care-only follow-up. Requires eGFR, slope, albuminuria, CKD cause, current management quality, existing nephrology access, transplant/dialysis planning relevance, and local access constraints before recommendation.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes mortality, emergency dialysis-start, and hospitalization prevention from an advancing-CKD referral-gap baseline risk, confounding-discounted early-referral effect, and internal D2 event_value_qaly. C2 education/symptom-control and C3 planned-transition value are honest zeroes pending separate sourced utility or residual-event inputs.

c2_capacity.basis: Honest zero: no separate CKD education, anxiety reduction, dietitian, symptom-control, or medication-optimization utility anchor is credited beyond C1 event prevention.

c3_resilience.basis: Honest zero: planned dialysis access, transplant preparation, and emergency-start severity reduction require a distinct residual-event/transition-quality model before C3 credit.

burden_qaly.basis: Per-year burden for nephrology visits, multidisciplinary dietitian/pharmacist/education contacts, repeated labs, travel/time, medication changes, dietary restrictions, procedure-planning anxiety, and care-fragmentation risk. Integrated over a 3-year referral-window horizon.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: all-cause mortality, emergency dialysis start, and serious CKD hospitalization avoided by early nephrology referral and multidisciplinary care in advancing CKD with a real referral-timing gap

basis.baseline_event_risk: Explicit three-year advancing-CKD referral-gap phenotype band for death, crash dialysis start, and serious CKD hospitalization; not a general CKD-population risk and not a live individualized kidney-failure model.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: Cheng 2025 early-versus-late referral meta-analysis HR 0.67 converted to central RRR 0.33; bands widen/downshift for confounding, heterogeneous definitions of early referral, and bundled downstream-care effects.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal D2 composite event value for mortality, emergency dialysis transition, kidney-failure morbidity, and serious CKD hospitalization, excluding separate downstream medication/procedure value.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Three-year referral-window horizon; burden is per-year over the same horizon.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly at declared cadence

cohort_projection.display_note: Narrow Batch 020 promotion; engine computes C1 from derivation_inputs and subtracts burden at the declared cadence and horizon.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands include CKD stage, eGFR slope, albuminuria, etiology, current management quality, local nephrology capacity, referral timing, transplant/dialysis-planning relevance, confounding in referral studies, internal event-value uncertainty, and visit/lab/travel burden.
overlap_rules: Do not double count renal, hospitalization, mortality, dialysis-transition, or transplant-preparation value with ACEi/ARB, SGLT2, finerenone, BP control, anemia management, transplant referral, dialysis-access planning, potassium-binder enablement, or CKD education records unless endpoint slices are partitioned.

Referral pathway value is incremental to care coordination and timing; direct value from medications or procedures triggered by nephrology remains with those downstream records unless a bundle explicitly reallocates it.

citation.source_text: Cheng L, Hu N, Song D, Liu L, Chen Y. Early versus late nephrology referral and patient outcomes in chronic kidney disease: an updated systematic review and meta-analysis. BMC Nephrol. 2025;26(1):25.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert HR 0.67 into central RRR 0.33, with a wide 0.15-0.45 band to reflect observational confounding and heterogeneous referral definitions.
evidence_trace.limitations: Referral is a bundled care-pathway exposure, not a blinded treatment; patient value requires a real counterfactual referral gap and avoids crediting downstream treatments twice.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind to an advancing-CKD, referral-gap phenotype with a three-year composite risk for death, emergency dialysis start, or serious CKD hospitalization before applying the early-referral effect.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal phenotype risk band pending a live CKD progression and crash-start risk model; not applicable to stable low-risk CKD or patients already in timely nephrology care.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use a D2 composite event_value_qaly band for mortality, emergency dialysis transition, kidney-failure morbidity, and serious CKD hospitalization while excluding downstream medication/procedure value.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal event-value model, not a direct trial utility measurement; confidence remains capped for model-translated value.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and explicit burden cadence.
evidence_trace.limitations: Generic event runner only; C2 education/symptom utility and C3 planned-transition resilience are not added without separate sourced inputs.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply per-year specialist and multidisciplinary-care burden over a 3-year referral-window horizon, including visits, labs, travel, education, dietary restrictions, care fragmentation, and procedure-planning anxiety.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration pending direct patient-time, acceptability, access, and adverse-experience data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Early nephrology referral has clinically coherent meta-analytic outcome evidence, but this valuation is medium-capped because absolute QALY is phenotype-bound, confounding-sensitive, model-translated, and internally burden-calibrated.

applies_when.interpretation: Narrow Batch 020 valuation for confirmed severe symptomatic aortic stenosis where valve replacement is clinically indicated and the counterfactual is no valve replacement or delayed/inaccessible valve intervention. Requires valve metrics, symptom status, EF and flow-gradient phenotype, frailty, STS/procedural risk, TAVR/SAVR candidacy, anatomy, life expectancy, patient goals, and competing noncardiac mortality before recommendation.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes mortality and heart-failure event prevention from untreated symptomatic severe-AS risk, AVR relative effect, and internal D2 mortality/HF event value. Symptom/function C2 and residual-event C3 are honest zeroes pending separate sourced utility and recovery inputs.

c2_capacity.basis: Honest zero: dyspnea, angina, syncope-risk anxiety, KCCQ/NYHA, exercise tolerance, and independence gains are not credited until a separate utility anchor is bound and de-duplicated from survival/HF-event value.

c3_resilience.basis: Honest zero: better recovery or lower residual HF-event severity after AVR is not independently parameterized.

burden_qaly.basis: One-time episode burden for valve workup, TAVR/SAVR hospitalization, periprocedural mortality/stroke/vascular or bleeding complications, pacemaker/AKI/delirium risk, early recovery and rehab, antithrombotic management, echo follow-up, surveillance, reintervention, and durability uncertainty.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: all-cause mortality and heart-failure events avoided by valve replacement versus no valve replacement in severe symptomatic aortic stenosis with appropriate procedural candidacy

basis.baseline_event_risk: Explicit one-year untreated or delayed-intervention symptomatic severe-AS risk band for mortality and serious HF events, anchored to the high-risk/inoperable PARTNER B-style counterfactual rather than all severe AS phenotypes.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: PARTNER cohort B HR 0.55 converted to central RRR 0.45; bands widen for TAVR/SAVR candidacy, frailty, competing noncardiac mortality, era, and procedural-risk differences.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal D2 event value for mortality and serious HF morbidity in severe AS, excluding separate symptom/function C2 and explicit procedural harms already represented in one-time burden.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: One-year evidence/counterfactual horizon for the high-risk no-valve-replacement comparison; longer durability and C2 symptom value remain blocked.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly at declared cadence

cohort_projection.display_note: Narrow Batch 020 promotion; engine computes C1 from derivation_inputs and subtracts the one-time procedure burden.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands include symptomatic status, valve severity phenotype, TAVR versus SAVR candidacy, frailty, STS/procedural risk, competing mortality, untreated-risk applicability, procedural complication burden, internal event-value uncertainty, durability, and overlap with HF, CAD, falls/syncope, and mortality records.
overlap_rules: Do not double count mortality, HF hospitalization, syncope/falls, CAD, BP, anticoagulation, or downstream HF-management value with valve intervention unless endpoint slices are partitioned.
Symptom/function C2 must remain separate from avoided HF events and survival value; procedural complications and recovery burden are counted once in the valve procedure episode.

citation.source_text: Leon MB, Smith CR, Mack M, et al. Transcatheter aortic-valve implantation for aortic stenosis in patients who cannot undergo surgery. N Engl J Med. 2010;363(17):1597-1607.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert HR 0.55 into central RRR 0.45 for the severe symptomatic, inoperable/high-risk no-valve-replacement counterfactual; widen to 0.30-0.55 for candidacy, era, frailty, and competing-risk uncertainty.
evidence_trace.limitations: PARTNER cohort B does not apply to asymptomatic severe AS, moderate AS, low-gradient phenotypes without careful adjudication, or patients whose procedural risk and goals make AVR nonbeneficial.

citation.source_text: Leon MB, Smith CR, Mack M, et al. Transcatheter aortic-valve implantation for aortic stenosis in patients who cannot undergo surgery. N Engl J Med. 2010;363(17):1597-1607.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind to a one-year untreated or delayed-intervention severe symptomatic AS event-risk band for mortality and serious HF events in a PARTNER B-style high-risk counterfactual.
evidence_trace.limitations: Explicit phenotype binding, not an all-comer AS risk model; contemporary standard care, procedural candidacy, frailty, and competing mortality may shift absolute risk.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use a D2 event_value_qaly band for severe-AS mortality and serious HF morbidity avoided, excluding separate symptom/function utility and explicit procedural harms.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal event-value model, not direct trial-measured utility; confidence remains capped for model-translated value.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and the declared one-time burden.
evidence_trace.limitations: Generic event runner only; symptom/function C2, durability, and residual-event resilience are not added without separate sourced inputs.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply one-time procedure-episode burden for valve workup, hospitalization, complications, recovery, rehab, antithrombotics, surveillance, reintervention, and durability uncertainty.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration pending patient-specific TAVR/SAVR risk, local outcomes, recovery, and preference data.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Severe symptomatic AS with a no-valve-replacement counterfactual has direct randomized survival evidence, but this narrow valuation is medium-capped because absolute QALY is phenotype-bound, model-translated, and internally burden-calibrated with C2 symptom value intentionally blocked.

applies_when.interpretation: Applies narrowly to confirmed impaired glucose tolerance or DPP-like high-risk prediabetes where acarbose is being considered as an incremental medication option after lifestyle and modern cardiometabolic risk management have been reviewed. Do not use for routine low-risk prediabetes, diabetes already requiring other therapy, or patients unlikely to tolerate meal-timed dosing or GI adverse effects.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes eligible-IGT cardiovascular composite baseline risk * STOP-NIDDM relative risk reduction * internal D2 cardiovascular composite event_value_qaly. Diabetes-delay, hypertension, weight, symptoms, and broader metabolic value are not credited.

c2_capacity.basis: Honest zero. No separate capacity, weight, glycemic-symptom, treatment-satisfaction, or anxiety credit is added without sourced utility anchors and endpoint partitioning.

c3_resilience.basis: Honest zero. Post-MI, stroke, HF, peripheral vascular, or diabetes-complication resilience is not independently parameterized.

burden_qaly.basis: Per-year acarbose burden covering meal-timed dosing, titration, flatulence, diarrhea, abdominal discomfort, adherence friction, medication review, A1c or glucose follow-up, hypoglycemia interaction checks, cost/access, and discontinuation reassessment.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: cardiovascular composite events in confirmed impaired glucose tolerance treated with acarbose, excluding diabetes-delay and hypertension-only value

basis.baseline_event_risk: Explicit eligible-IGT cardiovascular composite risk band over the STOP-NIDDM-style evidence horizon; this is not a general prediabetes default and should be replaced by patient-specific ASCVD/HF/peripheral-vascular risk when available.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: STOP-NIDDM HR 0.51 carried as central RRR 0.49, with a very wide 0.05-0.76 band for event-count imprecision, composite heterogeneity, old-standard-care context, adherence, and contemporary comparator uncertainty.
basis.event_value_qaly: Internal D2 cardiovascular composite event-value band for MI, stroke, HF, peripheral vascular, and cardiovascular death mix; diabetes-delay and hypertension effects are deliberately excluded to avoid overlap.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Approximate trial-follow-up horizon for annual medication burden integration; no post-trial durability extrapolation is credited.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly at declared cadence

cohort_projection.display_note: Narrow Batch 025 promotion; engine computes C1 from derivation_inputs and subtracts per-year medication burden over the declared 3.3-year horizon.

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands reflect IGT phenotype certainty, baseline cardiovascular composite risk, old STOP-NIDDM evidence, event-count imprecision, modern comparator therapy, medication adherence and discontinuation, GI intolerance, internal cardiovascular event-value mapping, and overlap with diabetes-prevention and cardiometabolic records. C2 and C3 are honest zeroes.
overlap_rules: Do not double count diabetes-prevention, weight-loss, BP, lipid, smoking, aspirin, GLP-1/GIP, SGLT2, metformin, lifestyle, or nutrition value unless endpoint slices are explicitly partitioned.
This promoted valuation credits only the cardiovascular composite event slice; diabetes-onset delay, hypertension prevention, and glycemic symptom effects remain excluded.
Shared medication review, glucose monitoring, GI adverse effects, adherence, and discontinuation burden must be assigned once across cardiometabolic medication records.

citation.source_text: Chiasson JL, Josse RG, Gomis R, Hanefeld M, Karasik A, Laakso M; STOP-NIDDM Trial Research Group. Acarbose treatment and the risk of cardiovascular disease and hypertension in patients with impaired glucose tolerance. JAMA. 2003;290(4):486-494.

evidence_trace.transformation: Convert STOP-NIDDM HR 0.51 to central RRR 0.49 for the cardiovascular composite; widen to 0.05-0.76 for precision, component mix, adherence, and contemporary standard-care uncertainty.
evidence_trace.limitations: Old RCT, small cardiovascular event count, composite heterogeneity, IGT-only eligibility, and modern cardiometabolic counterfactual uncertainty keep confidence low.

evidence_trace.transformation: Bind to a STOP-NIDDM-style high-risk IGT cardiovascular composite risk band over 3.3 years before applying the relative effect.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal baseline-risk binding; should be upgraded with patient-specific ASCVD, HF, peripheral vascular, age, smoking, BP, lipid, and diabetes-progression risk.

evidence_trace.transformation: Use an internal cardiovascular composite event-value object for MI, stroke, HF, peripheral vascular event, and cardiovascular death mix; exclude diabetes delay and hypertension-only outcomes.
evidence_trace.limitations: Model-translated composite value, not trial-measured utility; component-specific event values and fatal/nonfatal mix remain upgrade paths.

evidence_trace.transformation: C1 is computed by event_or_curve_shift_action from baseline_event_risk, relative_risk_reduction, event_value_qaly, and the declared medication-burden cadence.
evidence_trace.limitations: Generic event runner only; no separate C2/C3, diabetes-delay, hypertension, or post-event recovery value is credited.

evidence_trace.transformation: Apply per-year medication burden over the 3.3-year evidence horizon, including meal-timed dosing, GI adverse effects, monitoring, access, adherence friction, and discontinuation review.
evidence_trace.limitations: Internal burden calibration pending patient-specific tolerance, dose, cost, and discontinuation probability.

evidence_axis.basis_summary: STOP-NIDDM provides randomized relative-effect evidence for an IGT cardiovascular composite, but confidence remains low because absolute value is internally baseline-bound, model-translated, old-standard-care dependent, GI-burden sensitive, and endpoint-limited.

applies_when.interpretation: Established ASCVD secondary prevention antiplatelet candidate: prior MI, ischemic stroke/TIA, coronary revascularization, chronic coronary disease, symptomatic PAD, or documented ASCVD; exclude active major bleeding, aspirin allergy, concurrent anticoagulation unless specifically modeled, and active DAPT/other antithrombotic bundles that supersede aspirin monotherapy.

c1_prevention.basis: Runner computes baseline_event_risk * relative_risk_reduction * net_event_value_qaly. The event_value_qaly band is net of audited bleeding/intolerance harm debit collapsed into the current deterministic event schema because separate harm objects are not yet runner-supported.

c2_capacity.basis: No direct felt-quality channel credited apart from avoided events.

c3_resilience.basis: No separate resilience channel credited; stroke/MI sequelae are included in event-value modeling.

burden_qaly.basis: Small chronic daily oral-medication burden. Bleeding and intolerance harms are not placed here; they are collapsed into net event_value_qaly for this current-schema promotion.

derivation_inputs.target_outcome: recurrent serious vascular events (nonfatal myocardial infarction, nonfatal ischemic stroke, vascular death) in established ASCVD

net_harm_adjustment.schema_note: Current event_or_curve_shift_action runner has no separate harm-object subtraction. Audited aspirin harm debit is therefore incorporated into event_value_qaly rather than ignored.

basis.baseline_event_risk: Ten-year recurrent serious vascular-event risk for stable established ASCVD, using REACH central band with lower stable and risk-amplified bands; ATT secondary-prevention control risk retained only as a high-risk ceiling, not default.
basis.relative_risk_reduction: ATT secondary-prevention trials: serious vascular events 6.7% per year with aspirin vs 8.2% per year control, rounded RRR 0.19.
basis.event_value_qaly: Composite fallback event value for recurrent serious vascular events, reduced by audited aspirin bleeding/intolerance harm debit because the current runner cannot encode harm objects separately.
basis.sustained_horizon_years: Chronic secondary prevention modeled over a 10-year default horizon, stop/switch on major bleeding, anticoagulation, DAPT, or contraindication.

net_qaly_if_achieved.calculation: runner_computed: c1_prevention + c2_capacity + c3_resilience - burden_qaly.central * sustained_horizon_years

action_valuations.uncertainty_basis: Bands combine recurrent ASCVD baseline-risk uncertainty, ATT effect uncertainty, composite event-value uncertainty, explicit net-harm collapse, and chronic pill-burden band.
overlap_rules: Do not sum aspirin monotherapy and DAPT/P2Y12/dual-pathway antithrombotic benefits as independent full MACE reductions for the same event horizon.
Aspirin monotherapy is superseded or replaced during active DAPT, P2Y12 substitution, dual-pathway PAD therapy, or anticoagulation unless an explicit incremental model is present.
